Tim knew that his mom always took her big bag to meetings. Tim wanted to help his mom, so he thought of a plan. He would load her big bag with all the things she needed for her meeting. He put a pen, some paper, and a snack in the bag. He was very excited and couldn't wait to see his mom's happy face when she saw what he did. When it was time for the meeting, Tim gave his mom the big bag. She opened it and saw all the things Tim had put inside. She smiled and hugged Tim, saying "Thank you for helping me get ready for my meeting!" Tim felt so proud and happy that he was able to help his mom. From that day on, Tim always helped his mom load her big bag for meetings, and they both lived peacefully ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a creative little girl named Lisa. She loved to skip and play with her friends in the park. One sunny day, Lisa found a secret box under a big tree. She was very excited and wanted to find out what was inside. Lisa thought of a plan to open the secret box with her friends. They tried to use sticks, rocks, and even their hands, but nothing worked. Lisa had one more idea. She asked her friends to skip around the box and sing a special song. They all did as Lisa said, but still, the box did not open. Feeling sad, Lisa and her friends left the secret box under the tree. As they walked away, a mean boy found the box and took it home. He was not nice, and he did not share the secret box with anyone. The bad ending of this story teaches us that we should always work together and never give up.

It will be so much fun!" Bob agreed and said, "Yes, let's do it! I will help you organize the party." Together, they invited all their friends to the party. The animals came and ate the hard prunes. They played games and had a great time. The party was a big success. After the party, Sam and Bob were very happy. They thanked the big tree for giving them the hard prunes. The tree was also happy to see all the animals having fun. From that day on, Sam and Bob became the best of friends. Once upon a time, there was a young boy called Dave. Dave was wild and full of energy and loved to explore. One day, he stumbled upon an old, rusty spade. He knew it needed some repair, but he was too small to do it himself. Dave decided to ask his Dad for help. He soon discovered that his Dad was a master of repairs and could fix anything! So his Dad took the spade and soon it was shining like new. Dave was so happy with the result that he decided to explore the nearby woods. But the next day, when he was walking, Dave got stuck in a hole. He remembered the spade that his Dad had fixed and suddenly knew what to do. He grabbed the spade and started to dig himself out of the hole. The moral of the story is: 'Sometimes the tools we have can help us in unexpected ways. We should take care of things and make sure they're always in good repair.'

Anna and Ben are friends who like to play in the park. They see a pigeon on the grass and want to catch it. They run after the pigeon, but it flies away. They try again and again, but the pigeon is too fast and smart. "Let me catch it!" Anna says. "I want to win!" "No, let me catch it!" Ben says. "I want to win too!" They start to push and pull each other, and forget about the pigeon. They are not friends anymore. They are angry and sad. The pigeon sees them and feels sorry for them. It flies down and lands on a bench near them. It makes a soft sound and looks at them with its bright eyes. Anna and Ben stop fighting and look at the pigeon. They see that it is cheerful and friendly. They feel ashamed of their behavior. They walk slowly to the bench and sit next to the pigeon. They smile and say sorry to each other. The pigeon makes another sound and flaps its wings. It seems to say, "Good job! You are friends again!" Anna and Ben hug each other and thank the pigeon. They learn that winning is not as important as being kind and sharing. They play with the pigeon and have fun. They are happy and grateful.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to take a nap every day after playing with his toys. One day, while Tim was taking a nap, he had a dream. In his dream, he met a talking dog. The talking dog said, "Hello, Tim! I need your help to find the unknown number." Tim was surprised but wanted to help the talking dog. So, they went on an adventure together to find the unknown number. They looked under rocks, behind trees, and even in the sky. But they could not find the unknown number. Then, they met a wise old owl. The wise old owl said, "To find the unknown number, you must count all the stars in the sky tonight." Tim and the talking dog waited until night and started to count the stars. They counted and counted, but there were too many stars to count. Suddenly, Tim woke up from his nap. He realized it was just a dream, but he still wanted to find the unknown number. So, that night, he looked up at the sky and started to count the stars. He knew that the unknown number was somewhere out there, and he would keep looking for it.

Lily and Ben were twins. They liked to play outside in the snow. They made a big snowman with a hat and a scarf. They gave him a carrot nose and two rocks for eyes. "Hello, Mr. Snowman!" Lily said. "Do you want to play with us?" Mr. Snowman did not say anything. He just smiled with his carrot nose. Ben had an idea. He ran to the kitchen and got a big spoon. He came back to the snowman and said, "Look, Lily, I have a spoon. We can stir the snow and make it soft." Lily thought that was a fun game. She got another spoon and joined Ben. They started to stir the snow around the snowman. They made little hills and valleys with the snow. "Wow, this is so cool!" Ben said. "We can make a snow castle for Mr. Snowman." Lily agreed. She said, "Yes, let's make a snow castle. Mr. Snowman can be the king." They worked hard to make a snow castle. They used their spoons to make walls and towers. They used their hands to make windows and doors. They used their hats to make flags. They were very proud of their snow castle. They showed it to Mr. Snowman. "Look, Mr. Snowman, this is your castle!" Lily said. "You can live here and be happy." Mr. Snowman did not say anything. He just smiled with his carrot nose. Lily and Ben smiled too. They hugged Mr. Snowman and said, "We love you, Mr. Snowman. You are our friend." The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird who lived in a cage. The bird was angry because he wanted to be free. He did not like being in the cage all day. One day, he saw a cat outside the cage. The cat was big and mean. The bird said to the cat, "Please, let me out of the cage. I want to be free." The cat looked at the bird and said, "I will let you out if you give me something." The bird thought for a moment and said, "I can sing a pretty song for you." The cat agreed, and the bird sang a beautiful song. After the song, the cat opened the cage. The bird was happy to be free, but the cat was not nice. The cat demanded, "Now you must stay with me forever." The bird did not want to stay with the mean cat. They argued and the cat got angry. In the end, the cat caught the bird and the bird was never free again. The bird should have been more careful with who he trusted.

Once upon a time there was a small bunny who was very curious. One day, the bunny came upon a big strawberry. The bunny couldn't believe how big it was, so she decided to examine it. She was so scared that she stopped in her tracks and her ears twitched. Suddenly, a voice said, “Don’t be frightened, little bunny. It’s ok.” Then the bunny saw a friendly looking bear. The bear asked the bunny, “Do you want to examine the strawberry? I can help you.” The bunny was very happy and said, “Yes, please!” The bear showed the bunny how to examine the strawberry. He said, “See how big this strawberry is? It's amazing!” The bunny felt brave now and said, “Yes, it is big! Thank you for showing me how to examine it.” The bear smiled and said, “You’re welcome!” The bunny and the bear then said goodbye and continued on their way. The bunny was no longer frightened and learned an important lesson that day: when you’re curious, it’s best to examine things with a friend.

One day, Sara is playing with her dolls. She makes them have a tea party. She gives them cups and plates. She says, "Here you go, Anna. Here you go, Elsa. Do you like the tea? It is very yummy." She makes them nod and smile. Then, Sara hears a noise. She looks under her bed. She sees something moving. She is curious. She moves her dolls aside. She crawls under the bed. She discovers a sock. It is not her sock. It is a big sock. It is blue and green. It has stripes. It has a hole. She wonders who it belongs to. She picks up the sock. She hears another noise. She sees a tail. It is a furry tail. It is brown and white. It wags. She follows the tail. She finds a dog. It is a small dog. It is hiding under the bed. It looks scared. It shivers. It has no collar. It has no name. Sara feels sorry for the dog. She says, "Hello, dog. Are you lost? Are you cold? Do you want to be my friend?" She holds out the sock. The dog sniffs the sock. It licks the sock. You can keep it. You can also stay with me. I will take care of you. I will give you food and water. I will give you love. I will give you a name. How about Spot? Do you like Spot?" The dog nods. They will like you, too." Sara and Spot crawl out from under the bed. They join the tea party. They share the tea and the cake. They play and laugh. They are happy. They are friends.

The moral of the story is that it's important to mix up your activities and not do too much of one thing. Too much of anything can make you dizzy.

Once upon a time, there was a big hippo. The hippo was very honest. He had many friends because he always told the truth. One day, the hippo went to the pond to play with his friends. At the pond, the hippo's friends were making a lot of noise. They wanted the hippo to listen to their stories. The honest hippo listened to each friend's story. He liked to hear what they had to say. After listening to all the stories, the honest hippo told his own story. His friends loved to listen to him because they knew he always told the truth. They all played and laughed together at the pond. They were very happy to have an honest hippo as their friend.

Ben and Mia like to play with bricks. They have many bricks of different colors and shapes. They can build towers, houses, cars and animals with bricks. Sometimes they have a contest to see who can build the highest tower. One day, they are building towers in the living room. Ben has a red brick, a blue brick, a yellow brick and a green brick. He puts them on top of each other. Mia has a pink brick, a purple brick, a white brick and a black brick. She puts them on top of each other too. They both want to add one more brick to their towers. Ben sees a big orange brick on the shelf. He thinks it will make his tower very tall and strong. He stands on his toes and tries to reach the orange brick. But he is too short. He jumps and stretches his arm, but he still cannot reach the brick. He feels angry and frustrated. Mia sees a small brown brick on the table. She thinks it will make her tower very pretty and cute. She walks to the table and tries to reach the brown brick. But she is clumsy. She trips over a toy and falls on the floor. She drops her tower and it breaks into pieces. She feels sad and hurt. Ben and Mia look at each other. They see their broken towers and their unhappy faces. They feel sorry for each other. They decide to stop the contest and help each other. They hug and say sorry. They pick up the bricks and start to build a new tower together. They use all the colors and shapes they have. They make the tower very big and beautiful. They are happy and proud. They have fun with bricks.

Once upon a time, there lived a little girl named Lucy. One day, Lucy was sad and tired. She sighed loudly and asked her mom, "Why am I so tired?" Her mom put her arm around her and said, "Sometimes you feel tired when you don't get enough sleep." Lucy yawned and nodded. "But why? I want to play." Her mom smiled and said, "You can play, but first let's have some dinner. I made your favorite – spaghetti with sauce!" Lucy's face lit up. She hugged her mom tight and said, "Yay! I love spaghetti with sauce!" She happily ate her dinner and sighed with contentment. As she drifted off to sleep, Lucy knew she was never too tired to enjoy her favorite dinner.

The barber saw the child and he shouted, "Come over here and show me your pretty smile!" The child smiled and walked over. The tall barber then opened his big bag and took out a sharp pair of scissors. The child was scared and ran away crying. The tall barber then laughed and shook his head. He thought it was funny that the child was so scared. Sadly, the child did not have a happy time.

Lily was very scared. She had to go to the hospital for an operation. She did not like the hospital. It was big and noisy and smelled funny. She did not want to leave her mom and dad. "Mom, dad, please don't go!" she cried, holding their hands. "We won't go, sweetie. We'll be right here with you," mom said, giving her a hug. "But I don't want the operation. It will hurt!" Lily said, shaking her head. "The doctor will give you some medicine to make you sleep. You won't feel anything. The operation will make you better, Lily. You have a high fever and a bad ear. The operation will fix that," dad said, stroking her hair. Lily looked at the doctor. He was wearing a white coat and a mask. He had a big needle in his hand. He smiled at her, but she did not smile back. "Hello, Lily. I'm Dr. Lee. I'm going to help you with your ear. Are you ready?" he asked. Lily shook her head again. She squeezed mom's and dad's hands harder. She did not want to go with the doctor. "It's okay, Lily. You're very brave. We'll be right here, waiting for you. We love you very much," mom said, kissing her cheek. "We'll see you soon, Lily. You'll be fine. We're very proud of you," dad said, kissing her forehead. The doctor took Lily's hand and led her to a big bed with wheels. He put a mask over her nose and mouth. "Take a deep breath, Lily. Count to ten with me. One, two, three..." he said. Lily felt sleepy. She closed her eyes. She hoped the operation would be over soon. She hoped mom and dad would be there when she woke up. She hoped her ear would not hurt anymore. She hoped the doctor was nice. She hoped...

Once there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play ball with his friends. They played in the park every day. Tim was a good player, but he wanted to be the best. One day, a new kid came to the park. His name was Sam. Sam seemed very good at playing ball. Tim was worried. He thought, "Will Sam be a better player than me?" Tim wanted to find out, so he asked Sam, "Do you want to play ball with us?" Sam said, "Yes, I would love to play!" They played ball together, and it was easy for Sam. Tim watched Sam and learned new ways to play. Soon, Tim and Sam were the best players in the park. Tim was happy that Sam came to the park. They became good friends and played ball together every day. Tim learned that playing with others can make you a better player. And Sam learned that making friends is easy when you share the things you love.

The storekeeper smiled and said “Why don't you get the original pumpkin?” Charlie was delighted! He wanted the original pumpkin because it was the biggest and brightest pumpkin there. So he paid for it and took it home. When Charlie got home, his whole family was so happy. They all ate the pumpkin together and had a wonderful time. From then on, Charlie made sure to always get the original pumpkin, because it always made everyone happy.

Once, there was a boy who wanted to buy candy. He had saved his money and was excited to get something special. But the price of the candy was too high, and the boy was disappointed. The boy's father said, "It's foolish to buy candy at that price. Ignore it and let's look for something else." The boy replied, "But I want it!" The father said, "What you want doesn't always mean it's best for you. The price we have to pay for something doesn't always tell us the true value of it. Let's look somewhere else for something better." So the boy ignored the high price and the two of them looked for something else. They found something that the boy liked and that cost much less than the candy. The moral to this story is that sometimes we need to ignore what looks good at first and remember that the price we pay doesn't always tell us the true value.

Spot set out to explore the pit. He ran fast and jumped high. But when he got to the pit, he saw a big cat. The cat was not happy. The cat hissed and tried to chase Spot away from the pit. Spot was scared, but he wanted to see what was in the pit. Spot thought of a plan. He found a ball and threw it far away. The cat ran after the ball. Spot quickly looked inside the pit and found a big bone. He took the bone and ran back to his home. Spot was happy, and the cat was happy with the ball. They both learned to share the yard and became friends.

One day, a little bird named Tim was on a high tree. He saw a hanger on the ground. Tim wanted to play with the hanger, so he flew down to get it. As he picked up the hanger, he met a squirrel named Sally. Sally was sad. "Why are you sad, Sally?" asked Tim. "I can't find my nuts," said Sally. "I hid them, but now I can't find them." Tim wanted to help Sally. He had an idea. "Let's use the hanger to spring the nuts out of their hiding places," said Tim. They worked together, using the hanger to spring the nuts out of their hiding places. Soon, they found all of Sally's nuts. Sally was so happy, and she hugged Tim. "Thank you for helping me, Tim," said Sally. "You're a good friend." From that day on, Tim and Sally were best friends. They learned that helping each other made them happy. And they played with the hanger every day, finding new ways to have fun together. The moral of the story is: Helping others can make you happy and bring you new friends.

The little cloud rose higher and higher until it became a mighty cloud too. Now, the little cloud was big and strong, just like the mighty cloud. He would throw it high in the air and watch it rise. The ball made Tim very happy. But one day, he threw the ball too hard. It went into a tall tree. Tim was sad. He looked up at the tree and saw the ball stuck. He wanted to get it back. Tim thought of a plan. He found a long stick and poked the ball. The ball fell down, but it landed in a sour puddle. Tim picked up the ball and washed it. It was not sour anymore. Tim was happy again. He played with the ball, but this time, he did not throw it too hard.

Once upon a time, there was an ordinary leopard who liked to build things. Every day he would build something new, and soon he had a big collection of things he had built. He was so proud of his achievements! But then one day, while walking through the jungle, something strange happened. The leopard stepped on a branch and the branch broke under his weight. He was so startled that he tumbled backwards, toppling over all the things he had built. The leopard was very sad and he wondered why this had to happen to him. He soon realised that it was because he hadn't been careful. He had built his creations in a hurry and hadn't taken the time to make sure they were strong enough. And this was why they had been so easily destroyed. The leopard learnt an important lesson that day. He realised that he should take more care next time he builds something and be sure to make sure it will not break too easily. He now takes pride in making sure that his creations are strong and durable. The moral of the story is that taking your time and being careful will often result in much better things.

"Can I have one?". Mum replied, "Well, if you can find the right pattern then you can take one". Mary looked closely at the blocks trying to decide which pattern she should call out. After a while she saw a difficult pattern in the top right corner. She called out "That one!". Mum smiled and said, "Well done, that was very difficult!" Mary was so happy. She quickly put the block away in her pocket and ran around the garden. Mum and Mary had a lot of fun playing with the blocks. They both enjoyed finding the patterns so much that they played the game until it was time for dinner.

Once upon a time, there was a fast cat named Tom. Tom was very good at catching mice. He liked to play with them before he ate them. Tom's skin was soft and shiny. One day, Tom saw a big mouse. He wanted to catch it and play with it. But the big mouse was very smart. It knew that Tom needed food, so it said, "I will supply you with food if you don't catch me." Tom thought about it and said, "Okay, I will not catch you." The big mouse gave Tom food every day. But one day, the big mouse stopped giving Tom food. Tom was hungry and sad. He tried to catch the big mouse, but it was too late. The big mouse had many friends now, and they all helped it run away. In the end, Tom was left hungry and alone. The moral of the story is: Don't trust someone just because they give you something.

"I will count and you hide in the box." "OK," says Sam. He goes inside the box and closes the lid. Lily counts to ten. Then she looks for Sam. She does not see him. She hears a noise from the box. She opens the lid. "Boo!" says Sam. He jumps out and scares Lily. Lily laughs. She sees a paper in the box. She picks it up. It has words on it. It is a poem. "Look, Sam, a poem," says Lily. "Can you read it?" Sam shakes his head. He does not know how to read. "Me neither," says Lily. "But maybe we can make up our own poem." They sit on the grass and think of words that rhyme. They say them out loud. "Box, fox, socks," says Sam. "Park, bark, dark," says Lily. They giggle. They like making poems. A lady walks by. She sees the paper in Lily's hand. She stops and looks at it. "Hey, that's my poem," she says. "I wrote it for a contest. How did you get it?" Lily and Sam are surprised. They did not know the paper belonged to someone. "We found it in the box," says Lily. "We are sorry. We did not mean to take it." The lady is angry. She snatches the paper from Lily. She is bossy. "Give me that," she says. "You should not touch things that are not yours. And you should not make fun of poems. Poems are serious and important." Lily and Sam feel sad. They do not like the lady. They do not think poems are serious and important. They think poems are fun and silly. They run away from the lady. They find another place to play. They forget about the poem. They make up their own games. They are happy.

She asks her mom, "Where is Tom? Why is he not here?" Her mom says, "Tom is sick. He has a cold. He has to stay at home and rest. He will come back soon." Lily says, "I want to see Tom. I want to play with him. Can we go to his house?" Her mom says, "No, we can't. We have to keep away from Tom. We don't want to make him sicker. But we can do something nice for him. We can make him a present." Lily says, "A present? What can we make?" Her mom says, "We can make him a card. We can draw a picture of the park and write 'Get well soon, Tom'. We can also give him some cookies. He likes cookies." Lily says, "Yes, let's do that. I think Tom will like that. He will be happy." They go home and make the card and the cookies. They put them in a bag. They walk to Tom's house. They ring the doorbell. Tom's mom opens the door. She says, "Hello, Lily and Lily's mom. What a nice surprise. How are you?" Lily says, "We are good. We made a present for Tom. We miss him. We hope he gets better soon." Tom's mom says, "That is very sweet of you. Thank you. Tom will love your present. He misses you too. He is in his room. He can't come out. But you can wave to him from the window." Lily and her mom go to the window. They see Tom in his bed. He looks pale and tired. He smiles when he sees them. He waves back. Lily holds up the bag. She says, "This is for you, Tom. We made you a card and some cookies. We want you to swing high, slide fast and run around with us again." Tom says, "Thank you, Lily. You are a good friend. I like your card and your cookies. They make me feel better. I want to swing high, slide fast and run around with you again too. I will be well soon. I promise." Lily says, "OK, Tom. I will wait for you. I will see you in the park. Bye, Tom." Tom says, "Bye, Lily." Lily and her mom say goodbye to Tom's mom. They leave the bag at the door. They walk back to their house. Lily is still sad. But she is also happy. She knows Tom will be happy too. She knows they will play together again. She knows they are friends.

Once upon a time, there was a bird who wanted to soar in the sky. But the bird was so small that it was too scared to even try. The bird looked up to a fisherman who was sailing by and said, "It looks like so much fun to fly up in the sky!" The fisherman smiled and said, "If you want to reach the sky, you have to believe in yourself and try, try, try!" The bird was ready; it flapped its wings and took off into the air. Everyone cheered and praised the bird for trying and being fair. But then a big dark cloud rolled in and the bird got scared and flew away. The fisherman said, "Courage comes from believing in yourself, don't ever give up and you'll find it someday." The bird slowly built up its courage and it flew back. With the courage to try, the bird could now soar in the sky and the bird was oh so proud. The moral of the story is: Believe in yourself and you can do anything. Don't be scared of trying and always be fair.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird who loved to soar in the sky. Every day, she looked down on the world and dreamed of what it would be like to fly around the world. Every morning, the little bird soared up higher in the sky and thought of where she wanted to go. She admired the world below her and she wanted to explore all of it. One day, the little bird decided to go on a grand adventure. She was brave and decided to go to a nearby farm. When she arrived, she saw a lively squash patch. The squash were so colorful and inviting. The little bird sprung into action and scooped some of the squash off the vines. After a few minutes of gathering the squash, the little bird continued her journey. She flew with the squash all over the farm and admired the world from above. It was so peaceful and calming. As she flew around, she was excited to see what else the world had to offer. The little bird then took all the squash and soared off into the sky. Making her way across the world, she saw many different sights. She enjoyed the adventure and was so happy to be exploring the world. The little bird eventually ended her journey with a river of squash flowing behind her. She enjoyed the adventure and it taught her many things. She was able to explore the world and learn more about it. She also gained a greater appreciation of the lively squash she collected

Once upon a time, there was a tall tree in a big park. The tall tree had a coat of green leaves. One day, a small bird came to live in the tree. The small bird liked the tall tree's coat. But there was a problem. The small bird did not behave. It was loud and messy. The tall tree did not like this. The tall tree wanted the small bird to be quiet and clean. The tall tree talked to the small bird. It said, "Please behave. Be quiet and clean." The small bird listened to the tall tree. It started to behave. The small bird became quiet and clean. The tall tree and the small bird were happy. They lived together in the big park.

Once upon a time, there was a big, brown dog with soft fur. The dog loved to play with his red ball. One day, the dog was playing with his ball near a tree. While playing, the dog saw a sour apple on the ground. He thought it was a ball too, so he tried to play with it. But when he bit the apple, it did not feel like his red ball. It was sour and not fun to play with. The dog did not like the sour apple. The dog went back to his red ball and played with it again. But this time, he played too hard and the ball broke. The dog was sad, but he knew that he could find a new ball to play with. And he learned to stay away from the sour apples.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max loved to play on the road. One day, Max saw a big ball on the other side of the road. He wanted to get the ball, but he didn't look left or right before crossing. This was a terrible thing to do. As Max ran to get the ball, a big car came fast on the road. The car stopped just in time, and Max was safe. But Max felt bad and knew he did something wrong. He felt regret in his heart for not being careful. From that day on, Max always looked left and right before crossing the road. He learned that it is important to be careful and safe. And Max told all his friends to do the same. The unexpected thing was that Max became a hero for teaching everyone to be safe. The moral of the story is to always be careful when crossing the road.

Lucy and the cat walked together in the maze. They helped each other. Soon, they found the way out. Lucy and the cat were happy. They played in the park all day. They became best friends.

Once upon a time, in an ancient town, there lived a fireman named Tom. Tom was a very good fireman. He always helped people when there was a fire. One day, he saw a big fire in a house. He ran to the house and said, "I will put out the fire!" Tom worked hard to stop the fire. He used a lot of water to make the fire smaller. But the fire spread to another house. Tom was sad and scared. He did not know what to do. Then, he heard a voice from the sky. It was a big, strong bird. The bird said, "I can help you, Tom!" The big bird flew down and used its big wings to blow the fire away. The fire stopped spreading and went out. Tom was very happy and thanked the big bird. The people in the town were safe again. From that day on, Tom and the big bird became best friends and helped each other to keep the town safe.

Bella loved animals. She wanted to be a veterinarian when she grew up. She liked to read books about animals and ask questions to her mom and dad. One day, she went to the library with her mom. She saw a big book with a picture of a lion on the cover. She wanted to read it. She asked her mom, "Can I borrow this book, please?" Her mom looked at the book. It had many hard words that Bella could not read. She said, "This book is too difficult for you, honey. Why don't you choose another one?" Bella was frustrated. She really wanted to learn about lions. She said, "But I want to know how lions live, and what they eat, and how they roar. Please, mom, let me have this book." Her mom smiled. She had an idea. She said, "How about we go to the zoo tomorrow? There is a veterinarian there who takes care of the lions. Maybe he can answer your questions." Bella was happy. She said, "Yes, yes, yes! Thank you, mom. I can't wait to meet the veterinarian and the lions." The next day, they went to the zoo. They saw many animals, but Bella was most excited to see the lions. They were big and beautiful and loud. She saw a man wearing a white coat and a hat. He was holding a clipboard and a stethoscope. He was the veterinarian. Bella ran to him. She said, "Hello, sir. I want to be a veterinarian like you. Can you tell me about the lions?" The veterinarian smiled. He said, "Sure, little girl. I'm happy to answer your questions. What do you want to know?" Bella asked him many questions. He answered them all. He told her how lions live in groups called prides, how they hunt and eat other animals, how they communicate with roars and purrs, and how he checks their health and treats their wounds. Bella listened with wonder. She learned many new things. She thanked the veterinarian. She said, "You are very nice and smart. I want to be like you when I grow up." The veterinarian said, "You are very curious and brave. I think you have what it takes to be a veterinarian. Keep reading and asking questions, and maybe one day you will be." Bella hugged him. She said, "Thank you, thank you, thank you. You made my day." Her mom watched them with a smile. She was glad she took Bella to the zoo. She knew Bella would never forget this day. She knew Bella would always love animals.

One day, a flea named Tim was very excited. He wanted to drive a big car. So, he jumped on a car and found a nice spot to sit. Tim was happy and ready for his big adventure. As the car started to move, Tim shouted, "Wee! I am driving a big car!" He was having so much fun. But then, a big wind came and blew Tim away from the car. He tried to hold on, but he was too small. Tim landed on the ground, far away from the car. He was sad and cried, "I don't want to drive anymore." The car drove away without him, and Tim was left all alone.

Lila and Ben are at the zoo with Mom and Dad. They see many animals, like lions, monkeys, and birds. But their favorite animal is the elephant. The elephant is big and gray, and has a long nose called a trunk. "Look, Mom, the elephant is waving at us!" Lila says, pointing at the trunk. "Wow, you're right, Lila. He is very friendly," Mom says, smiling. "Can we wave back, Mom?" Ben asks. "Of course, Ben. Let's wave and say hello to the elephant," Mom says. Lila and Ben wave their hands and shout, "Hello, elephant!" The elephant waves his trunk again and makes a loud sound. Lila and Ben giggle. "Maybe he is saying hello, too," Ben says. "Maybe he is, Ben. Or maybe he is hungry," Mom says. "Can we feed him, Mom?" Lila asks. "Well, we have to ask the zookeeper first. He knows what the elephant likes to eat," Mom says. They look around and see a man wearing a fancy hat and a shirt with the zoo logo. He is holding a big bucket of fruits and vegetables. "Excuse me, sir, can we feed the elephant?" Mom asks. "Sure, you can. He loves apples and carrots. Here, take some from the bucket and hold them out for him. He will use his trunk to grab them," the zookeeper says. He gives Lila and Ben some apples and carrots and shows them how to feed the elephant. Lila and Ben are very happy. They feed the elephant and watch him eat. The elephant is happy, too. He waves his trunk again and makes another loud sound. "Thank you, elephant. You are very nice," Lila and Ben say. "Thank you, kids. You are very kind," the zookeeper says. Lila and Ben hug Mom and Dad and say goodbye to the elephant. They wave one more time and walk to see the next animal.

He became very sick. His tummy hurt a lot. The cat said, "I told you, Tim." Tim learned that he should listen to his friends and not eat filthy things.

Once upon a time, there was a big helicopter. It was red and shiny. One day, the helicopter saw a fake bird in the sky. The helicopter wanted to talk to the bird. The helicopter said, "Hi, bird! Let's be friends!" But the bird did not talk back. The helicopter was sad. It did not know the bird was fake. The sky turned dark, and rain started to fall. The helicopter was scared. It wanted to find a friend to help. Then, it saw the fake bird again. The rain made the bird split in two! The helicopter saw it was a fake bird. It felt better, knowing it was not a real friend. The rain stopped, and the helicopter found a real bird to be friends with. They played in the sky, happy and free.

He will never be bad." One day, while Tim and Bob were playing, a big wind came and blew Bob away. Tim was very sad and missed hugging his friend. He looked everywhere for Bob, but could not find him. Then, something unexpected happened. A little girl found Bob and gave him a hug. She saw Tim's name on Bob's tag and knew she had to help Bob find his friend. The little girl brought Bob back to Tim. Tim was so happy to see Bob again. He hugged the little girl and said, "Thank you for helping Bob find me." The little girl smiled and said, "You're welcome! I knew Bob wasn't a bad bear. He just needed a hug." From that day on, Tim, Bob, and the little girl became best friends, and they all hugged each other every day.

Sue smiled and said, "Thank you." Tim and Sue became good friends. They played under the tree every day. They would lean on the tree and laugh together. They both learned how to jog around the tree without falling down. The tree was happy to see Tim and Sue play together. The tree knew that friends help each other when they feel embarrassed. It had a picture of a rainbow and her name in big letters. She ran to the front of the class with her page in her hand. But she tripped on a toy and fell down. Her page flew out of her hand and landed on the floor. The other kids laughed at her. Lily felt very embarrass. "Are you okay, Lily?" her teacher asked. She helped her get up and picked up her page. She saw the rainbow and the name. She smiled and said, "This is a very nice page, Lily. You did a great job. Do you want to share it with the class?" Lily nodded. She was still a little embarrass, but she was also proud of her page. She held it up and said, "This is my page. I made a rainbow and wrote my name. I like rainbows and my name. Do you like it?" The other kids stopped laughing and looked at her page. They saw the bright colors and the big letters. They clapped and said, "Yes, we like it. It is very pretty. You are very good at drawing and writing, Lily." Lily smiled. She felt happy and not embarrass anymore. She thanked her teacher and went back to her seat. She put her page in her backpack. She decided to show it to her mom and dad when she got home. She thought they would like it too.

The bird thought for a moment and said, "I can help you look for your toy. I can fly high and see things from up there." The girl was happy and said, "Thank you, bird!" The bird flew high in the sky with its thick wing. It looked all around and saw the toy under a big tree. The bird flew back down and told the girl, "I found your toy! It's under the big tree." The girl was so happy and hugged the bird. They went to the big tree and got the toy. The girl and the bird became good friends and played together every day.

Lucy wanted to show her mom how she could reverse in the water. She said, "Mom, watch me!" Lucy walked backward into the water, away from the jellyfish. Her mom clapped and said, "Great job, Lucy!" She hugged Lily and stayed near her mom. Then, a boy came over and looked at Lily. He said, "What a silly doll! Why does she wear sunglasses? It's not sunny today!" He grabbed Lily's sunglasses and ran away. Anna was very angry and sad. She cried, "Give them back! They are mine!" Her mom heard her and said, "Don't worry, Anna. We'll get them back." She followed the boy and asked him to return the sunglasses. The boy was scared and said, "Sorry, I was just joking. Here, take them." He gave the sunglasses back to Anna's mom. Anna's mom gave the sunglasses back to Anna and said, "There you go, sweetie. Now Lily can see again." She kissed Anna and Lily and said, "You are both very brave and strong. Do you want to play with the other kids now?" Anna nodded and smiled. She put the sunglasses back on Lily and said, "Come on, Lily. Let's go have some fun." She wrapped Lily in a blanket and walked to the swings. She saw a girl who smiled at her and said, "Hi, I'm Mia. Do you want to swing with me?" Anna said, "Hi, I'm Anna. This is Lily. Yes, please." She picked it up and started to speak into it. "Hello, cookie store? I want to order a big cookie, please," she said. Lily was happy with her plan. Just then, her mother walked into her room with a big smile. She was holding a plate with a big cookie on it! "I heard you talking to the cookie store, so I decided to make you a cookie after dinner," her mother said. Lily was very surprised and happy. Lily liked to study books with pictures and words. She wanted to learn many things. She had a neighbor named Tom who also liked to study books. They sometimes studied together and shared their books. One day, Lily found a big book with many pictures and words. It was about animals and plants and places. She was very happy and wanted to show it to Tom. She ran to his house and knocked on the door. "Tom, Tom, look what I have!" she said, holding the book. Tom opened the door and saw the book. He was impressed by the book. He wanted to see it and study it. "Wow, Lily, that is a very impressive book. Can I see it?" he asked. "Sure, Tom, we can study it together. Come on, let's go to my house," Lily said. They went to Lily's house and sat on the floor. They opened the book and looked at the pictures and words. They saw lions and elephants and giraffes. They saw mountains and rivers and forests. They saw many things they did not know. They were so busy studying the book that they did not hear Lily's mom calling them for lunch. They did not hear Lily's dad coming home from work. They did not hear Lily's dog barking. They only heard a loud noise when Lily's dad stepped on the book by accident. He did not see the book on the floor. He was angry and shouted. "What is this? Who left this book here? This is a very expensive book. It belongs to my boss. He lent it to me for a project. How could you be so careless?" Lily and Tom were scared and sad. They did not know the book was not Lily's. They did not know the book was very expensive. They did not know the book belonged to Lily's dad's boss. They only wanted to study the book and learn. They tried to say sorry, but Lily's dad did not listen. He took the book and threw it in the trash. He told Lily and Tom to go away and never touch his things again. Lily and Tom ran away crying. They felt bad and ashamed. They lost the book and their friend. They did not want to study books anymore.

Sue was happy because she found a match for her napkin. Everyone at the party clapped for Sue and her napkin. Sue's mom was very proud of her.

Sara and Ben laugh and clap and try to catch the bubbles. One day, they see a big truck on the street. The truck has a long hose and a big tank. The truck stops near their house and a man gets out. He wears a mask and gloves and carries a tool. He goes to a pipe on the wall and connects the hose. He turns a knob and gas comes out of the tank and goes into the pipe. Sara and Ben are curious. They wonder what the man is doing. They think the gas might be like bubbles. They want to join the man and see the gas. They run towards the truck and the hose. The man sees them and shouts. He is angry and scared. He tells them to stop and go away. He says the gas is not like bubbles. He says the gas is dangerous and can hurt them. He says the gas can make fire and smoke and noise. Sara and Ben are fearful. They do not like the man's voice and words. They do not like the gas and the hose. They do not want to join the man and see the gas. They run away from the truck and the hose. They go back to their house and their bottle and their wand. They blow into the wand and make many bubbles. The bubbles are round and shiny and float in the air. Sara and Ben laugh and clap and try to catch the bubbles. They are happy and safe.

One day, Mr Gray was looking at the blackboard in his classroom. Everyone was observing him silently because he looked worried. He announced to the class that it was going to be a very icy day. The students wondered what this meant, so the teacher explained: "It means we are all going to help each other to keep warm. If you see something that will help someone else stay warm, be sure to do it." The students all agreed and for the next few days, they did whatever they could to help each other. Some shared coats, others shared blankets, and even some of the younger students gave away pieces of their lunch, like sandwiches and apples. One day it was time to go home, and all the students were outside, saying goodbye before they headed out to their warm homes. Suddenly, they noticed Mr Gray standing out there too. He had stayed out in the icy weather, patiently waiting for his students. He had brought them all mittens, so they would stay warm when they were walking home. The students all smiled, because they had learned a valuable lesson that day. By being kind to one another and showing concern for others, you can help each other and do more than you ever thought was possible.

One day, Lucy saw something move under the sheet. She thought it was a harmless bug. She was not scared. She wanted to poke it and see what it was. Lucy reached out her finger and poked the sheet. But, it was not a bug! It was Lucy's cat, Mittens! Mittens jumped out from under the sheet and meowed. Lucy was very surprised. She laughed and played with Mittens. They had fun with the sheet together.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a little bird named Billy. Billy had an impressive nest that he shared with his parent. One day, Billy's parent went out to search for food. While his parent was away, Billy decided to fly around the forest. He met a squirrel named Sally. "Hi, I'm Sally," said the squirrel. "What are you doing?" Billy replied, "I'm just flying around while my parent searches for food." Sally asked, "Can I help you search, too?" Billy agreed, and they both started searching for food. They found some yummy berries and nuts. When Billy's parent returned, they were very happy to see all the food Billy and Sally had found. From that day on, Billy and Sally became best friends. They always helped each other search for food and had lots of fun together in the big forest. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, Mia met a talking bee named Buzz. Buzz saw that Mia was sad and asked, "Why are you sad, Mia?" Mia told Buzz about her lawn with no flowers. Buzz wanted to help Mia and said, "I can help make your lawn pretty with flowers." Buzz went to find flower seeds and brought them to Mia. They planted the seeds together in the lawn. Mia was excited and waited for the flowers to bloom. After a few days, the flowers started to bloom, and Mia's lawn was full of pretty colors. Mia was no longer miserable. She thanked Buzz and they became best friends. They spent their days playing in the beautiful lawn with blooming flowers.

He heard a tiny voice say, "I can help you find the herb!" Tim looked around and saw a small bug. The bug was fit and strong. Tim asked, "Can you really help me find the herb?" The bug said, "Yes, but first, you must help me move this big rock." Tim and the bug tried to move the rock, but it was too heavy. They did not give up. They pushed and pushed until the rock moved. The bug was happy and said, "Thank you, Tim! Now, let's find the herb." Together, they found the herb. Tim picked it and said, "Thank you, little bug!" Tim went home and used the herb to make his food taste better. He was happy he had a new friend and they had solved their problem together.

Sara was a big girl who liked to do things by herself. She was independent and did not need help from anyone. She liked to play with her toys, read her books, and draw with her crayons. She did not like to nap or rest, because she thought it was boring. One day, her mom came to her room and said, "Sara, it is time to relax. You have been playing all day, and you need to rest your body and mind. Come, let me read you a story and give you a message." Sara did not want to relax. She said, "No, mom, I do not want to relax. I want to play more. I am not tired. I am independent. I can do what I want." Her mom smiled and said, "I know you are independent, Sara, and I am proud of you. But everyone needs to relax sometimes, even big girls like you. Relaxing is good for you. It helps you grow, learn, and be happy. A message is when I rub your back, your arms, and your legs gently. It feels nice and makes you calm. A story is when I read you a book with pictures and words. It makes you imagine and dream. Do you want to try?" Sara thought for a moment. She liked when her mom read her stories and gave her messages. She remembered how cozy and warm it felt. She decided to give it a try. She said, "Okay, mom, I will relax. But only for a little bit. Then I want to play again." Her mom nodded and said, "Deal. Come, lie down on your bed and close your eyes. I will read you a story and give you a message. You will see how good it feels to relax." Sara did as her mom said. She lay down on her bed and closed her eyes. Her mom read her a story about a princess and a dragon. She gave her a message on her back, her arms, and her legs. Sara felt her muscles relax and her mind drift. She felt happy and peaceful. She did not want to play anymore. She wanted to relax. She said, "Thank you, mom. I like to relax. I like your story and your message. You are the best mom ever." Her mom kissed her forehead and said, "You are welcome, Sara. I love you. You are the best daughter ever. Now, sleep well and have sweet dreams."

Tim pressed the button with the number 5 on it. The elevator went up, and it made a "ring" sound when it stopped. They got out of the elevator and walked down a long hall. Tim heard a harsh noise coming from a room. He was scared. His mom said, "Don't worry, Tim. It's just a machine making that noise. The doctor is nice and will make you feel better." They went into the room, and the doctor checked Tim. After they were done, they went back to the elevator and went home. Tim was happy and not scared anymore.

The caterpillar looked at her and said, "Yes, I want to be your friend. But first, you have to choose the real me from two caterpillars. One is me, and one is fake." Lily looked and saw another caterpillar that looked just like her new friend. Lily thought very hard. She looked at both caterpillars. She saw that one was moving a little bit, and the other one was not moving at all. She pointed to the moving one and said, "You are the real caterpillar!" The caterpillar smiled and said, "You are right, Lily! I am the real one. The other one is just a toy. Thank you for choosing me. Now we can be friends forever!" And they played and laughed together in the park all day long.

The lion and the bird became best friends. The lion was not fierce anymore, and all the animals wanted to be his friend.

Tom was a big boy who liked to help his dad. One day, his dad was chopping wood with an axe. Tom wanted to try too. He asked his dad, "Can I use the axe, please?" His dad said, "No, Tom, the axe is too sharp and heavy for you. You might hurt yourself or someone else. You can help me by picking up the small sticks and putting them in the pile." Tom was sad. He wanted to chop wood like his dad. He thought he could manage. He waited until his dad was busy with something else. Then he sneaked to the axe and grabbed it. He lifted it with both hands and swung it at a big log. But the axe was too sharp and heavy for Tom. He lost his balance and fell down. The axe flew out of his hands and landed on his foot. Tom screamed in pain. His dad heard him and ran to him. He saw the axe and the blood on Tom's foot. He was very scared and angry. He picked up Tom and carried him to the car. He said, "Tom, what have you done? You were very naughty and foolish. You could have lost your foot or worse. You should have listened to me. Now we have to go to the hospital." Tom was very sorry and ashamed. He cried and said, "I'm sorry, dad. I wanted to chop wood like you. I thought I could manage. But the axe was too sharp and heavy for me. It hurt me a lot. Please don't be mad at me." His dad said, "I'm not mad at you, Tom. I'm just worried about you. I love you very much. But you have to be careful and obey me. The axe is not a toy. It is a tool for grown-ups. You are still a little boy. You have to learn and grow before you can use it." They went to the hospital. The doctor looked at Tom's foot. He said, "You are lucky, Tom. The axe did not cut your bone or your nerve. You only need some stitches and a bandage. You will be fine in a few weeks. But you have to rest and keep your foot clean. And you have to promise me that you will never touch the axe again." Tom nodded and said, "I promise, doctor. I will never touch the axe again. I learned my lesson. I will be a good boy and help my dad in other ways. Thank you for fixing my foot." The doctor smiled and said, "You're welcome, Tom. You are a brave boy. I hope you feel better soon." Tom thanked the doctor and hugged his dad. His dad hugged him back and said, "I'm proud of you, Tom. You are a good boy. I'm sorry I yelled at you. I was just scared. I'm glad you are fine. I love you very much." Tom said, "I love you too, dad. And I'm sorry I disobeyed you. I will listen to you from now on. You are the best dad in the world." They went home and Tom rested on the couch. His dad gave him some juice and a cookie. He read him a story and sang him a song. Tom felt happy and safe. He knew his dad was right. The axe was not for him. He would wait until he was big enough to use it. He closed his eyes and fell asleep.

Once upon a time, there were two intelligent ducks who lived in a pond. They had a lot of fun, quacking and swimming together. One day, they found that a big wreck had shown up in their pond. The wreck was made of wood and was blocking the water. The two ducks started quacking away, trying to figure out what to do. "We need to fix this wreck," one duck said. "Let's work together to move it," said the other. So, the ducks worked together and moved the wreck away from the pond. From that day on, they had even more fun swimming and quacking together. The moral of the story is that two heads are better than one. Working together we can accomplish things that no one can do on their own.

One day, a girl named Sue was very excited. She was going to the museum with her mom. Sue loved the museum. It had big animals and fun things to see. She could not wait to get there. When they got to the museum, Sue saw a big dinosaur. She wanted to touch it. Her mom said, "Sue, do not go too far. Stay close to me." Sue said, "Okay, mom," but she did not listen. She ran to the dinosaur and touched it. When she looked around, her mom was gone. Sue started to panic. She did not know where her mom was. She was scared. Then, she saw a big door. It had a sign that said "Do not enter." Sue thought maybe her mom was inside. She opened the door and went in. Inside the room, Sue found a big surprise. There was a birthday party for her! Her mom was there, and so were all her friends. Sue was so happy. Her mom said, "I wanted to surprise you, but I did not mean to scare you. I am sorry." Sue said, "It's okay, mom. This is the best day ever!"

Once upon a time, there was a little flea. The flea lived in a big sack. The sack was on a soft bed. The flea was always ready to jump and play. One day, the flea met a new friend. The new friend was a tiny bug. The tiny bug wanted to play in the sack too. The flea said, "Come play with me in the sack. It is fun!" The tiny bug was happy and jumped into the sack. But the tiny bug was not a good friend. The tiny bug bit the flea. The flea was sad and hurt. The flea had to leave the sack. The flea learned to be careful when choosing friends.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play near the stream. The stream had amazing, clear water. Tim liked to watch the fish swim in it. One day, Tim met a girl named Lily. Lily was playing by the stream too. They became friends. Tim said, "Let's part the water and make a path for the fish!" Lily agreed, and they started to part the water with their hands. As they played, the sky turned dark and it started to rain. The rain made the stream grow bigger and bigger. Tim and Lily got scared. They tried to run away, but the water was too fast. The stream took them away, and they were never seen again.

It was sad, but that was the bad ending. The family had to find a new place to live.

There were photos of his mom, dad, and his dog, Spot. Tim was very happy to see these photos. He wanted to save them and show them to his friends. Tim took the photos to his friend's house. They looked at the photos and laughed. They saw photos of Tim when he was a baby, and photos of his dog, Spot, when he was a puppy. Tim was glad he saved the photos. They had a lot of fun looking at them together. And after that, they all felt a little sleepy too.

Once upon a time, there was a big, heavy elephant named Ella. Ella liked to look for things. One day, she went to look for a job. Ella went to the farm to look for a job. She saw a farmer with a big, heavy bag of apples. The farmer asked, "Can you help me, Ella? This bag is very heavy." Ella used her long nose to lift the heavy bag. The farmer was happy and said, "Good job, Ella!" Ella went home feeling proud. She had done a good job helping the farmer. From that day on, Ella would look for more jobs to help her friends. And everyone loved Ella for her kind heart and strong nose.

Sue had an idea. She gave the boy her shoes. They danced together and became friends. The unexpected thing was that Sue's mom saw them on TV. They were dancing and helping others. Sue and the boy became famous for their kind hearts. The moral of the story is to be kind and help others.

The bird had a big piece of ice in its beak! The bird dropped the ice in front of Tim and Sue. They were very happy and thanked the bird. Lily felt frustrated because she really wanted to play with the ball. Just then, a boy came running from a nearby house. He picked up the ball and said, "Hi, I'm Tom. Do you want to play with my ball in the park?" Lily smiled and said, "Yes, please!" So, Lily, her mom, and Tom went to the park to play with the ball. Once upon a time, there was a naughty crocodile. He lived in a big river. He liked to disturb the other animals when they tried to drink water. One day, the crocodile saw a small bird near the river. He thought it would be fun to disturb the bird. He swam very quietly, so the bird would not see him. He was ready to splash water on the bird. But, something unexpected happened. The small bird saw the crocodile and flew away. The crocodile was sad because he could not disturb the bird. He swam back to his home in the river. The next day, the crocodile saw the small bird again. This time, he did not want to disturb the bird. He just wanted to be friends. The crocodile said sorry to the bird for being naughty. The bird forgave the crocodile, and they became good friends. From that day on, the crocodile was nice to all the animals and did not disturb anyone.

Once upon a time, there was a poor little dolphin named Dolly. She lived in the big blue sea. Dolly was sad because she was all alone. She wanted to find a friend to play with. One day, Dolly saw a big group of dolphins swimming together. She swam up to them and said, "Hi, I am Dolly. Can I play with you?" The other dolphins looked at her and said, "No, you are too small and poor. You cannot play with us." This made Dolly very sad. Dolly swam away and found a nice turtle named Timmy. Timmy saw that Dolly was sad and asked, "Why are you sad, Dolly?" Dolly told Timmy about the other dolphins who did not want to play with her. Timmy said, "Don't worry, Dolly. We can be friends and play together." So, Dolly and Timmy played together and had lots of fun. They knew that being poor or small did not matter. They were happy to be friends and not separate.

The bird nodded, and Lucy picked up the nest. She put it back in the tree with the baby birds inside. The tiny bird thanked Lucy, and they all played with the big, red ball together.

Once upon a time, there was a clumsy dog named Spot. Spot loved to sort things. He had a big chest at home where he kept all his toys. Every day, he would take out his toys, play with them, and then sort them back into the chest. One day, Spot's mom told him that they were going to move to a new house. Spot was very excited, but he knew he had to be careful with his chest. He didn't want to lose any of his toys. So, Spot and his mom packed up the chest and put it in the moving truck. When they got to their new house, Spot was so happy to see his chest. He carefully opened it up and started to sort his toys. But, he noticed that one of his favorite toys was missing. Spot was sad, but he remembered that he had seen it before they moved. He went back to the old house and found his toy under a tree. Spot was so happy to have his toy back, and he knew that being careful and sorting his things helped him find it.

Little Tommy wanted a pet. He had seen all of his friends with them and he wanted one just like them. Every day he would beg and urge his mom and dad, but they never seemed to listen. One day, when Tommy was out playing in the garden, he heard a strange sound coming from the shed. Curiosity got the better of him, so he tip-toed over to take a better look. Inside the shed was a big, bald eagle! Tommy ran away in excitement. He couldn't wait to tell his mom! He urged her to let him keep the eagle, but his mom was concerned and didn't want it around the house. However, after a lot of convincing, she eventually gave in. Tommy was very happy and could talk of nothing else. He made sure to look after the eagle and bring it food every day. One day, the bald eagle thanked Tommy with a single word, and Tommy realized it was all worth it!

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play in the garden. They see many plants and flowers. They also see a big box with dirt. Anna says, "What is in the box?" Ben says, "My mom says it is radishes. She plants them for salad. Do you like radishes?" Anna says, "I don't know. I never try them. Can we see them?" Ben says, "Yes, but we have to be careful. We can't pull them out. They need to grow more." They go to the box and look inside. They see some green leaves sticking out of the dirt. They touch them gently. They wonder how the radishes look under the dirt. Suddenly, they see something move. They hear a loud noise. They jump back and scream. They see an enormous radish pop out of the box. It is bigger than them. It has a face and eyes and a mouth. It says, "Hello, I am Rudy the radish. I am the biggest and best radish in the world. Who are you?" Anna and Ben are scared and surprised. They don't know what to say. They run away from the box and the radish. They go to Ben's mom and tell her what they saw. She laughs and says, "Don't worry, it is just a joke. It is not a real radish. It is a toy that I made for you. I wanted to surprise you and make you laugh. Do you want to play with Rudy the radish?" Anna and Ben look at each other. They are not scared anymore. They are curious and happy. They say, "Yes, we want to play with Rudy the radish. He is funny and enormous. Thank you, Ben's mom. You are very clever and kind." They go back to the box and say hello to Rudy the radish. They have fun with him and the other plants. They learn more about radishes and salad. They also try some radishes and like them. They are crunchy and tasty. They are glad they have a new friend in the garden.

"This is a special surprise for you Tommy," She said with a smile. "It's an enormous toy car!" Tommy ran to the huge box and began to take out the parts. He called for his mom to help him put it together. When it was ready, Tommy and his mom put a battery in the car, and then he pressed the button on the car. It started moving! Tommy was so excited that he started laughing. He ran around the car and shouted with joy. He was having so much fun! Suddenly, the car stopped and Tommy looked confused. "Why did it stop?" he asked his mom. "That's because it needs another battery," she said. Tommy's face lit up and he got a new battery to put in the car. Then he took off and he was driving his enormous toy car again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Sarah. She was three years old, and her mom and dad said that she was perfect. Each day, Sarah saw posts in the back yard of her house, with flowers around them. Every week, they got new posts. Sarah asked her mom what they were, and her mom said that soon Sarah would find out. One sunny day, the posts got flowers again, but this time they had something special. Sarah's mom said that the posts were for her, and that she had to forgive something. Sarah didn't know what she had to forgive, but she hugged all the posts with the flowers around them. That night Sarah and her family had dinner together. They ate with special hearts and forgave each other. After dinner, Sarah understood why the posts were there - they were for her to show that mom and dad loved her, and that it was ok to forgive. Her mom and dad were so proud of how perfect Sarah was, and how she could forgive so easily.  From that day on, Sarah and her family always forgave each other. The posts in the garden were always a reminder of that.

She ate the whole fruit and it made her feel so happy and full. She said thank you to the tree and thanked the sky too. Then she shook the tree again, and another purple fruit fell down. She knew it was going to be just as delicious!

Once there was a generous kitten. Its name was Mittens and it was always ready to share its food and toys with its friends. One day, Mittens found itself in a place it had never been before. Everything was frozen. The trees were frozen and the grass was frozen. The air was so cold that Mittens was afraid it might freeze too. Mittens decided to search for a warm and cozy place to stay. After walking for a while, Mittens saw a beautiful house with a fireplace. Mittens went inside and lay down in front of the fireplace to get warm. But then, something unexpected happened. As Mittens was lying in front of the fireplace, the owner of the house appeared. She was not too pleased with the intruder. She said, "What are you doing here? This is my house and I don't want you here." Mittens was frightened. But quickly, it remembered it was a generous kitten. It explained that it was lost and just needed a warm place to stay. The woman listened to Mittens kindly and said "Ok, you can stay here tonight. But tomorrow, you must find somewhere else to go." Mittens nodded gratefully and settled down near the fireplace. From that day onward, the woman was generous to Mittens and even shared her dinner with it many times. So Mittens was safe from the cold and never had to worry about freezing again.

He hit the rock with the stick, but the rock did not break. Tom tried again and again, but the rock was too strong. After a while, Tom's friends came to the park. They saw him trying to split the rock and wanted to help. Together, they all pushed and hit the rock. Finally, the rock split into two pieces. Tom was so happy, he blew his whistle to celebrate. His friends were happy too, and they all played together for the rest of the day.

One day, Bob saw a clay pot on the ground. He walked over and picked it up. It felt soft and smooth. Bob looked up and saw his Mom. "Mom, what is this?" "That's clay, son. It's something special. When it gets wet it turns hard. You can use it to make all kinds of things." Bob smiled and said, "I trust it." Bob and his Mom went home. Bob put the clay pot in a safe place. Then he spent the rest of the day playing with his friends. That night, Bob remembered all the things his Mom told him about clay. He went back to get the pot and felt something wet. He realized he'd forgotten to put it away and it had gotten wet in the rain. Bob didn't know what to do. But he trusted his Mom's words and decided to wait and see what happened. The next morning, Bob looked inside the clay pot. It had turned hard. Bob found that something he trusted in had worked out just like his Mom had said. Bob was sure now that he could trust clay.

Tim and Mia went to the park with Mom. They saw many ducks in the pond. Tim and Mia liked the ducks. They wanted to feed them some bread. "Can we feed the ducks, Mom?" Tim asked. "Yes, but be polite and gentle. Don't throw the bread or scare the ducks," Mom said. Tim and Mia nodded. They broke the bread into small pieces and walked to the edge of the pond. They saw a big duck with a green head and a yellow beak. He looked hungry. "Hello, duck. Do you want some bread?" Mia said. The duck quacked and swam closer. Mia tossed a piece of bread to the duck. The duck caught it and ate it. He quacked again and wagged his tail. "Thank you, duck. You are very polite," Mia said. The duck nodded and smiled. He liked Mia. He wanted more bread. "Can I feed him too, Mia?" Tim said. "Sure, Tim. Here, take some bread," Mia said. Tim took some bread and threw it to the duck. The duck ate it and quacked. He liked Tim too. He wanted to be friends. "Thank you, Tim. You are very polite too," the duck said. Tim and Mia smiled. They clapped their hands and laughed. They were happy. They made a new friend. Mom watched them and smiled too. She was proud of Tim and Mia. They were polite and kind to the duck. She took a picture of them and the duck. She wanted to remember this day.

One sunny day, a little bird came to the owl's house. The bird was sad because it did not know how to clap. The wise owl thought about how to help the little bird. The owl looked in the dictionary and found the word "clap". The owl read the word to the little bird and showed it how to clap. The little bird practiced clapping with the help of the wise owl. Soon, the little bird could clap very well. The wise owl was happy to help the little bird. They both clapped together and had a fun time. The little bird was not sad anymore, and they became good friends.

She thought it looked like a good place to sketch. As she was doodling, she heard a whisper. She was surprised. She looked around to find out who was talking. Cautiously, she asked "Hello? Who is there?". To her surprise, a tiny fairy flew out from behind the tree. The fairy said "Hello, Lilah! I've been here for a long time, watching you draw." Lilah gasped. She never knew fairies existed! The fairy smiled and continued to whisper. "I was so impressed with your drawings! Keep going and never stop believing in your dreams!", the fairy said before flying away. Lilah smiled. She thanked the fairy and continued to draw with her pencil. She always remembered what the fairy said and kept drawing every day.

The bug was eating her mint plant. Mia tried to stop the bug, but it was too strong. The bug ate all the leaves, and the mint plant died. Mia was lonely again, and she cried.

Once upon a time, in an ordinary town, there lived a little duck named Ducky. Ducky had a very long neck, and all the other ducks would laugh at him. They would say, "Look at Ducky's long neck! It's so funny!" This made Ducky very sad. One day, a big bird came to the town. The big bird saw Ducky and said, "I hate your long neck!" Ducky felt even sadder. But then, a wise old duck came to Ducky and said, "Don't be sad, Ducky. Your long neck is special, and it can help you do things that other ducks can't." Ducky listened to the wise old duck and started to use his long neck to help others. He could reach high places and find food for his friends. Soon, all the other ducks stopped laughing at Ducky and started to like him. They learned that it is not nice to hate or laugh at someone just because they are different. In the end, Ducky and the other ducks lived happily together. They learned that everyone is special in their own way, and it is important to be kind to one another. And that was the moral value of the story.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy cat named Tom. He lived in a small house with his friend, a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to play with Tom, but sometimes Tom was too sleepy to play. One day, Sue was making sauce for her lunch. She put the sauce in a bowl and went to get a spoon. While she was gone, Tom woke up and saw the sauce. He was very hungry, so he went to the bowl to eat the sauce. But the sauce was hot! Tom jumped back and knocked the bowl over. The sauce spilled all over the floor. Sue came back and saw the mess. She was not mad at Tom. Instead, she laughed and said, "Let's clean this up and go play outside!" So they cleaned up the sauce and went outside to play. Tom was not sleepy anymore, and they played all day. Tom learned to speed around the yard, chasing butterflies and having fun with Sue. And they lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a dependable dog named Ben. Ben loved to sit on his chair all day. One day, a sneaky cat came and tried to steal Ben's chair. "Hey!" said Ben. "That's my chair! Give it back!" The cat just laughed and ran away with the chair. Ben was sad and didn't know what to do. He needed his chair back. Ben went to his friend, a big bird named Sam. "Sam, can you help me get my chair back? The cat took it!" Sam nodded and said, "Yes, Ben. I will help you." They went to find the cat. They found the cat sitting on Ben's chair. "Please give me back my chair," Ben asked the cat. The cat looked at Ben and said, "Okay, I'm sorry." The cat gave the chair back to Ben. Ben was very happy to have his chair back. He thanked Sam for being a good friend. From that day on, Ben and Sam played together and the cat never tried to steal Ben's chair again.

Once upon a time, there was a little yellow fish named Fin. Fin loved to swim all day long. One day, Fin saw something strange. It was a set of stairs going up and up. Fin wanted to see what was upstairs. Fin swam up the stairs, even though he knew fish should not leave the water. At the top, he found a big room, and in the middle was a huge bowl of water. Fin was so happy to swim in the big bowl. But then, something unexpected happened. A little girl came into the room and saw Fin. She was scared of fish and started to cry. Fin did not want the girl to be sad. He quickly swam back down the stairs and into his river. The little girl stopped crying and smiled at Fin. They waved goodbye to each other, and Fin knew he had made a new friend.

He was so excited that he wanted to take a few things from the port. So, Max carefully took out a few colorful items he liked. Just then Max heard a voice. It was the port's owner who said, "Max! What do you think you are doing? You can't just take things from my port!" Max started to feel scared, so he quickly put back everything and ran away as fast as he could. The port's owner chased him, but Max was too fast. He felt relieved and ran back home. From that day on, Max knew not to take things from other people's ports.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his red rod. He would put it in different places and pretend it was a magic wand. One day, Tim found a spicy pepper in the kitchen. He thought it would be fun to put the pepper on the rod and see what happens. Tim put the spicy pepper on the rod and started to play with it. Suddenly, the rod began to glow, and a tiny fairy appeared! The fairy said, "Thank you for freeing me from the spicy pepper! I will grant you one wish." Tim was very surprised, but he wished for a big, yummy cake. The fairy waved her wand, and a big, yummy cake appeared. Tim was so happy, and he shared the cake with his family. The fairy told Tim that by sharing the cake, he showed kindness and love. The moral of the story is to always share with others and be kind, and magical things can happen.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there lived a little mushroom. The mushroom was very shy and liked to observe the world around it. It saw many animals like birds, squirrels, and rabbits playing together. One day, a big, rude bird came to the forest. The bird saw the little mushroom and said, "Hey, you! Why are you just sitting there? Come and play with us!" The mushroom was scared and didn't know what to say. The other animals in the forest saw the rude bird talking to the mushroom. They came to help the mushroom and said, "Don't be rude to our friend! The mushroom likes to observe and learn from us." The rude bird felt bad and said sorry to the mushroom. From that day on, the bird was kind to everyone, and the little mushroom continued to observe and learn from its friends in the forest.

Once upon a time, in a big field, there was a lot of wheat. The wheat was tall and yellow. Some little mice lived in the field. They liked to eat the wheat. One little mouse was very adventurous. He liked to play and run in the wheat field. One day, the adventurous mouse wanted to gain more wheat for his family. He ran and ran through the field, picking up wheat with his tiny hands. He was very happy and excited. He wanted to show his family how much wheat he could get. When the adventurous mouse came home, his family was very happy. They had a big feast with all the wheat he had gained. The little mice laughed and played together, full and happy. The adventurous mouse was proud of his hard work and loved helping his family.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily had a big box full of toys. Her favorite toy was her amazing doll named Daisy. Lily loved to play with Daisy all day long. One day, Lily found a roll of tape in her daddy's toolbox. She thought it would be fun to play with. So, she asked her mommy, "Can I play with the tape, please?" Her mommy smiled and said, "Yes, I permit you to play with the tape, but be careful not to make a mess." Lily was so happy. She took the tape and played with Daisy. She used the tape to make dresses for Daisy and even made a little house for her. Lily and Daisy had a great time playing together with the tape. At the end of the day, Lily put the tape back in the toolbox and gave Daisy a big hug.

Once upon a time there was a chicken who was scared. He didn't know where to go, so he just ran around in circles. Eventually, he heard a voice calling out to him. "Follow me," said the voice. The chicken looked up and saw a little boy. He wasn't scared anymore. He followed the boy. The boy smiled and said, "Let's go find something fun!" The chicken felt better now. He kept following the boy, wherever he went. They went to the playground, but the chicken couldn't play on the swings or the slides. The boy just laughed and said, "That's okay, we can still have fun!" So, the boy and the chicken spent the day playing tag and chasing each other around the park. By the end of the day, the chicken wasn't scared anymore. He knew that the boy would take care of him.

Sue was eating a yummy pastry. Tim said, "Hi, I'm Tim. Can I play with you?" Sue smiled and said, "Yes, I'm Sue. We can play together." Tim and Sue played and laughed all day. They shared the pastry and became good friends. They were happy and had lots of fun. And from that day on, they always played together at the park.

One day, there was a naughty little girl named Jill. She liked to play with her rag dolls all day and she never wanted to listen to her mum. Her mum always said that if she wasn't careful, she would spoil her rag dolls. But Jill didn't care. She kept on playing with her dolls and thought that it would never happen. One day, something difficult happened. She accidentally dropped one of her dolls into a puddle! She had spoiled her doll and she was so sad. Jill's mum was very upset. She worried that Jill wouldn't learn her lesson if she didn't do something. So she decided to take away all of Jill's dolls. Jill was really upset but she eventually realised that she should take care of her toys. From then on, Jill understood that she should always be careful when playing with her dolls, so that she never has to spoil them again.
