The unknown made Sofia feel a bit nervous. She said, "Mama, what's school?" Her mom smiled and said, "It's a very special place where you can learn lots of things. You'll make new friends too!" Sofia was still feeling a bit unsure, so her mom said, "Don't worry, I will come with you." Sofia was happy when she heard this and smiled. The next day Sofia and her mom arrived at school. To her surprise, there were other kids her age playing in the playground and laughing. For the first time, Sofia felt excited about school. Her mom said goodbye and left her with a smile, saying, "Come back home when school is done." She gave Sofia a big hug, and that was when Sofia knew coming to school was the right thing to do.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was an enthusiastic kid who loved to have fun. He would always keep a big smile on his face. One sunny day, Tim went to the park with his mom. At the park, he saw a little girl named Sue. Sue was sad and sitting alone. Tim wanted to share his fun with her. Tim went up to Sue and said, "Hi! I'm Tim. Do you want to play with me?" Sue looked up and smiled. "Yes! That sounds like fun," she replied. They played together all day, and Sue began to feel happy too. As they played, Tim and Sue became good friends. They promised to keep having fun together and always be enthusiastic. And from that day on, Tim and Sue were always happy and full of joy.

Lily and Ben were playing with blocks in the living room. They liked to build towers and bridges and castles. They used a ruler to measure how tall and long their blocks were. "Look, Lily, my tower is 12 inches high!" Ben said, holding the ruler next to his tower. "That's good, Ben, but mine is 13 inches high!" Lily said, showing her tower to Ben. They smiled and kept building. They did not hear the wind outside getting louder and louder. Suddenly, they heard a loud noise and saw the windows shake. They looked outside and saw a big, dark, spinning thing. It was a tornado. It was coming towards their house. They were scared. They dropped their blocks and ran to their mom, who was in the kitchen. "Mom, mom, there's a tornado!" they cried. Mom hugged them and said, "Don't worry, my loves, we'll be safe. Come with me to the basement. We have a special room there. It's calm and strong. The tornado can't hurt us there." She took them by the hand and led them to the basement. She closed the door and turned on a flashlight. She gave them some blankets and toys and told them a story. They felt calm and safe with mom. They waited for the tornado to go away.

Bella and Mommy walked along the street, looking for something special. Then Bella saw some lovely fur in a shop window. "Mommy, look!" she said. Mommy smiled. "That's very interesting, Bella. Shall we buy it and celebrate?" Bella was so excited. She couldn't wait to take the fur home. When they got home, Mommy helped Bella make a pretty hat with the fur. They both wore their hats, then danced and sang and celebrated. Bella loved the fur and had lots of fun. Eventually, Bella decided it was time for her to go to bed. As she drifted off to sleep, she thought about how interesting, and fun, the fur was.

Tim and Sue wanted to play in the backyard. They liked to run and jump and swing on the big tree. But they had a problem. There was a big fence around the backyard. The fence was very high and tight. They could not climb over it or crawl under it. They asked their mom if they could go outside. Mom said no. She said there was a mean dog on the other side of the fence. The dog did not like children. He would bark and bite and chase them. Mom said they had to stay inside and play with their toys. Tim and Sue were sad and bored. They did not want to play with their toys. They wanted to play in the backyard. They had an idea. They found a big box in the garage. They cut a hole in the box. They put the box over the fence. They thought they could use the box as a tunnel to get to the other side. They pushed the box over the fence. They climbed into the box. They crawled through the hole. They were very happy. They thought they were very clever. They did not listen to their mom. They did not see the mean dog. The mean dog saw them. He was very angry. He did not like strangers in his backyard. He ran towards them. He barked and bit and chased them. Tim and Sue were very scared. They tried to run back to the box. But the box was too far. The dog was too fast. The dog caught them. He hurt them. He made them cry. Mom heard them cry. She ran outside. She saw the box. She saw the dog. She saw Tim and Sue. She was very worried. She called for help. She tried to get the dog away. She tried to get Tim and Sue back. It was very hard. It took a long time. Tim and Sue were hurt. They had cuts and bruises and bites. They had to go to the hospital. They had to get stitches and bandages and medicine. They were very sorry. They wished they had listened to their mom. They learned a lesson. They learned that they should not go where they are not supposed to go. They learned that they should not disobey their mom. They learned that they should not be too curious. They learned that curiosity can be dangerous.

The dog wagged its tail and barked happily. The barber and the dog became good friends. They played together every day, and the barber always served food to the dog. They were both very happy.

Once upon a time there was a jolly bear. It loved to play in the forest. One day, it found an alarm and it thought it was a fun toy. So, it took the alarm and started to mark with it. The bear made lots of marks in the mud and grass nearby. The alarm was not happy, so it started to sound. The bear got scared and ran away. As it ran, the alarm followed and kept making noises. By now, the bear was very scared. It ran as fast as its little legs could go, but the alarm never stopped. Soon, the bear was so tired that it could not run anymore. The alarm took this chance to catch the bear, and it gradually got closer and closer. Eventually, it caught the bear and the bear's jolly attitude was gone. The bear had to stay with the alarm forever.

Jenny was so happy when her mommy said, “You can use the computer now!” She had never seen such an attractive thing. Jenny couldn't believe she was getting to use the computer. She was too excited to think. Jenny's mommy had kindly set up the computer for Jenny. She could now use it and explore the world of computers. Jenny was so grateful to her mommy and happy that she had eaten her dinner.

Once upon a time, there was a little lion named Leo. Leo loved to play with his friends in the jungle. One day, Leo found a whistle on the ground. He picked it up and blew into it. It made a loud sound like a train. Leo was very happy with his new toy. Leo showed his whistle to his friends. They all wanted to try it. But Leo was scared that if he shared his whistle, it might break. So, he hid it under his bed. At night, Leo could hear the whistle calling him. It wanted to be played with. The next day, Leo heard a loud roar in the jungle. It was a big, scary lion. All of Leo's friends were scared and didn't know what to do. Leo remembered his whistle. He ran back to his bed and got it. He blew the whistle as loud as he could. The big, scary lion was scared of the whistle's sound and ran away. Leo and his friends were safe, and they all cheered. From that day on, Leo shared his whistle with his friends, and they all played happily together.

Once upon a time, there was a man who loved to think. He would sit by a deep pond and think all day long. One day, while he was thinking, he saw a little fish in the pond. The fish was sad and could not find its way home. The man wanted to help the fish. The man said to the fish, "Don't worry, little fish. I will think of a way to help you get home." The fish was happy and thanked the man. The man thought very hard and came up with a plan. He found a big leaf and put the fish on it. He then gently pushed the leaf across the pond to help the fish get back to its family. The fish was very happy to be home and thanked the man again. The man was glad that he could help the fish. From that day on, the man and the fish became good friends. The man learned that it is good to think and help others. And the fish learned that it is nice to have a friend who cares. The moral of the story is that thinking and helping others can bring happiness and friendship.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She had a toy box full of toys, but she always wanted more. One day, she found a long string on the ground. She thought it was a magic string, so she picked it up and tied it to her toy box. Amy asked the string to make her toy box always full, so she would never be bored. The string tried to help Amy, but it was not magic. The toy box stayed empty. Amy was sad, but she did not give up. She asked her mom to help her find more toys to fill the box. Amy's mom told her that she did not need more toys. She said that Amy could have fun with the toys she already had. Amy listened to her mom and played with her old toys. She found that she could have fun with them if she used her imagination. The string remained tied to the box, reminding Amy that she did not need more toys to be happy. The moral of the story is that sometimes, we already have what we need to be happy. We just need to use our imagination and be thankful for what we have.

Chirpy said, "Gigi, you should talk to our friends. They like you and want to hear your voice." Gigi was scared, but she wanted to try. She insisted, "Okay, Chirpy, I will try to talk to them." So, Gigi and Chirpy went to their friends. Gigi took a deep breath and said, "Hi, friends!" All her friends were so happy to hear Gigi talk. They all played and talked together, and Gigi was not shy anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play in the park with her friends. One day, while playing, she found a small, icy pond. The pond was so cold that it made her shiver. Lily saw a wise old owl sitting on a branch near the pond. The owl told Lily that if she drank the water from the icy pond, she would gain wisdom. Lily was curious and wanted to be wise, so she decided to try it. She cupped her hands and drank the icy water. As she drank, she felt a warm feeling inside her. From that day on, Lily became the wisest girl in the park. She helped her friends and shared her wisdom with everyone. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a brave girl called Sarah. She had a special sword that she always carried with her. One day, Sarah came across a big dragon. They both stared at each other for a while, and then the dragon said 'I'm hungry. Do you want to get me something to eat?' Sarah was a little embarrassed, but she said bravely 'Sure'. So she ran to the nearest shop and bought the dragon a big piece of cake. The dragon gobbled it up in one bite, and thanked Sarah. Sarah smiled and said 'You're welcome. It was nice to meet you'. She then pulled out her sword and held it up in the air. 'This is the best sword in town, and it's my secret weapon', she said proudly. The dragon smiled and said 'Well, it looks like you don't need me for protection anymore. I should go now'. With that, he flew off into the sky. Sarah sheathed her sword and watched the dragon go proudly. She felt strong, brave and most of all, not embarrassed.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Anna. She was three years old and loved to play outside. One day as she was playing in the garden she saw something strange. It looked like an umbrella but it was poking out of the ground. Anna was very confused and she wanted to know what was happening. So, she walked up to the umbrella and poked it with her finger. Suddenly, out popped a worm! Anna was so surprised that she jumped back and shouted "Eek!" Then she looked around and saw the worm crawling away. She chased after it, trying to catch it. But, the worm was too fast for Anna. She chased it for a long time but it kept getting away. Anna was beginning to feel very frustrated and confused. She was so determined to catch the worm that she kept trying until eventually, she managed to grab it. Anna was so happy that she had finally caught the worm that she ran straight home and showed her parents. They were so happy for her and gave her a big hug. Anna was very relieved that the mystery of the umbrella was solved.

You can have that apple!” Sam picked up the apple and took a big bite. He smiled from ear to ear. The apple was juicy and sweet. But then, something felt strange. The apple suddenly tasted weak and sour. Sam felt sick. His mom held him in her arms and said, “It sounds like that apple was not very good, Sam. We should not eat it anymore. It’s not very safe.” Sam nodded in agreement. He threw away the apple and they both went home.

He walked and walked for a long time, but he just couldn't seem to find what he was looking for. He sat down to take a break and began to feel a little sad. That's when he noticed a little blue bird, perched nearby. The bird hopped closer and chirped at Jake in a friendly way. Jake smiled and decided to ask for help. He asked the bird if it could remember what he was looking for. The bird thought for a moment and then chirped another friendly chirp. It hopped a bit closer and pointed a wing at a forget-me-not flower, growing in the field. Jake realised that the flower was what he had been looking for. He thanked the helpful bird and continued on his way.

One day, Sally went to the beach with her mom. Sally brought a bucket with her. She ran over to the shoreline and filled it with sand. She was so excited to build her first sandcastle. "Mom, can you help me make a tower?" Sally requested. Mom smiled and said, "Let's do it together!" Together, Sally and Mom built a tall tower. It was so high they could see the lighthouse in the distance. They both smiled, proud of their hard work. Just then, a grumpy seagull appeared. He flew over to Sally and squawked loudly. Sally got scared and stepped back. "Mom, I think he wants something!" Mom laughed, "I think he's just looking for food. Remember, never feed wild animals!" But the seagull squawked again, this time flying over to the bucket. He then stretched out his neck and pulled out a piece of sandwich Sally had forgotten. Sally gasped, embarrassed, but mom just smiled. "See, he just wanted something to eat! Let's just finish our sandcastle and go home." The seagull flew away, happy with his sandwich. Sally and her mom finished the sandcastle and went home with a smile, happy that Sally's simple request had been fulfilled.

She said, "Let's add our toys to the box and play together." The big dog liked the idea. So, they put their toys in the box and played together under the hot sun. They were happy and kind to each other again.

Once upon a time, there was a lazy cat named Tom. Tom loved to sleep all day and did not like to help his friends. One day, his friend, a little bird, reminded Tom to turn off the faucet after he drank water. But Tom did not listen. The next day, Tom went to drink water again. He forgot what the little bird said and left the faucet on. Water spilled everywhere, making a big mess. The little bird was sad and flew away. In the end, Tom's home was wet and dirty. He lost his friend, the little bird, because he did not listen. The moral of the story is to always listen to your friends when they remind you to do something good.

He decided to serve the bone to his other friend, a bird named Sally. Sam went to Sally and said, "Sally, I have a hard bone for you. I want to serve it to you." Sally looked at the bone and said, "Thank you, Sam. But I don't eat bones. They are too hard for me too." Sam was very sad. Sam and Ben are friends who like to play in the garden. They dig in the dirt and look for worms. They put the worms in a jar and watch them wiggle. One day, they find a big, fat worm that is longer than their fingers. Sam wants to keep it, but Ben wants to share it. They start to pull on the worm, each trying to get more of it. "Stop it!" Sam says. "This is my worm! I found it first!" "No, it's mine!" Ben says. "I helped you dig it out!" The worm starts to stretch and snap. Sam and Ben hear a loud pop and see two pieces of worm fall to the ground. They both gasp and let go of the worm. "Oh no!" Sam says. "We broke the worm!" "That's bad!" Ben says. "The worm is hurt!" They feel sorry for the worm and want to help it. They look around and see a bird on a branch. The bird is alert and hungry. It sees the worm and flies down to get it. "Watch out!" Sam says. "The bird is coming!" "Run, worm, run!" Ben says. "Hide in the dirt!" But the worm is too slow and the bird is too fast. The bird grabs the worm and flies away. Sam and Ben are sad and angry. They lost their worm and the bird got it. They look at each other and realize they made a mistake. They should have shared the worm and been nice to it. They should have thanked the worm for being their friend. They hug each other and say sorry. They promise to be better friends to each other and to the worms. They hope the worm is okay and the bird is happy. They go back to the garden and look for more worms. They find some and put them in the jar. They are gentle and careful with them. They thank the worms for being their friends. They are happy and alert. They play in the garden until it is time to go home.

The king looked at the green frog and thought about it. The king said, "I do not like green, but I will be your friend." The frog was so happy! They played and had fun together. One sunny day, Tom was going to work when he saw a big turtle in the road. The turtle was very slow and was delaying Tom. He decided to help the turtle cross the road. Tom picked up the turtle and put it on the other side. But then, something unexpected happened! The turtle started to talk! "Thank you, Tom," said the turtle. "I am a magic turtle, and I will give you one wish for helping me." Tom was very surprised. He thought for a moment and wished for a day off from work to spend with his family. The turtle granted his wish, and Tom had a great day with his family.

Sara and Ben are twins. They like to play with their toys in the garden. One day, they find a big ball of wool near the fence. It is soft and white and has many strings. "Look, Ben, we can make something with this wool," Sara says. She picks up the ball and tries to pull a string. But the string is weak and breaks easily. Ben shrugs. He does not like wool. He likes cars and trucks and planes. He sees a red car on the grass and runs to get it. "Ben, come back! Help me with the wool!" Sara calls. She wants to make a scarf or a hat or a doll. She thinks wool is fun and warm. But Ben does not listen. He plays with the car and makes loud noises. He pretends the car is fast and strong and can go anywhere. Sara feels sad and lonely. She puts down the ball of wool and walks to Ben. She tries to take the car from him. "Give me the car, Ben! It is my turn to play with it!" Sara says. "No, it is mine! Go away, Sara! You have the wool!" Ben says. They tug and pull and fight over the car. The car is weak too and breaks in half. Sara and Ben both cry. They look at the broken car and the ball of wool. They feel sorry and silly. They hug and say sorry to each other. "Let's share, Sara. You can have the wool and I can have the car. We can make something new with them," Ben says. "OK, Ben. That is a good idea. We can be friends and have fun," Sara says. They smile and go back to the garden. They use the wool and the car to make a funny toy. They call it a wool-car. They laugh and play with it until it is time to go inside.

Lila and Sam are friends. They like to walk in the park and see the animals. One day, they see a big bird with many colors. It is a parrot. "Hello, parrot!" Lila says. "What is your name?" The parrot looks at Lila and Sam. It opens its mouth and says something. But Lila and Sam do not understand. The parrot speaks a different language. "What did it say?" Sam asks. "I don't know," Lila says. "Maybe it said hello back. Or maybe it said something else." They try to talk to the parrot again. But the parrot only says the same thing. It is a loud and strange sound. "Maybe we can learn its language," Sam says. "Maybe we can say what it says." They listen to the parrot and try to copy its sound. They make loud and funny noises. The parrot listens to them and makes more noises. It sounds like they are talking. Lila and Sam laugh. They think the parrot is funny. They think they are learning a new language. They are happy. They walk away with the parrot. They say goodbye to the parrot in its language. The parrot says goodbye to them in its language. They are friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys. One day, she found a purple box with different shapes inside. She did not know what to do with them. Lily asked her mom, "Mom, what are these shapes for?" Her mom smiled and said, "These shapes are for you to learn and have fun, dear." Lily started to play with the shapes. She learned that the round shape was a circle and the pointy shape was a triangle. She played with the shapes all day and had lots of fun. One day, Lily's friend, Tim, came over to play. Tim did not know about the shapes. Lily taught Tim about the circle and the triangle. They played together and had a great time. The moral of the story is that sharing your knowledge with others can make you both happy and help you make friends.

Let's enjoy the sun together!” So Timmy and Lucy took off their pants and enjoyed the warm weather together. It was a fake boat made of wood and paper. The duck had made it to trick the fox, who often tried to catch him. The duck knew the fox could not swim very well. Soon, the water soaked the boat and it started to sink. The fox was scared and wet. He cried for help. "Help, help, I am sinking!" The duck heard him and swam to him. He was angry and said: "Fox, you are silly and mean. You took my fake boat and now you are in trouble. Why did you do that?" The fox felt sorry and said: "Duck, I am sorry and sad. Lily and Ben were hungry. They wanted to eat waffles for breakfast. But Mom was still sleeping. Lily and Ben decided to make waffles by themselves. They were careful not to wake up Mom. They went to the kitchen and found the waffle maker. They plugged it in and turned it on. They opened the fridge and took out the waffle mix and the syrup. They poured some waffle mix into the waffle maker and closed it. They waited for the beep. But they did not hear the beep. They smelled something burning. They opened the waffle maker and saw a black waffle. It was not good. They threw it in the trash. They tried again with more waffle mix. But they put too much. The waffle mix spilled out of the waffle maker and made a mess on the counter. "Oh no!" Lily said. "We have to clean this up!" They grabbed some paper towels and wiped the counter. But they did not see the syrup bottle. It was on the edge of the counter. Ben bumped into it and it fell down. The syrup splashed on the floor and made it sticky. "Oops!" Ben said. "We have to clean this up too!" They got more paper towels and tried to mop the floor. But they did not notice the cord of the waffle maker. It was on the floor too. Lily stepped on it and stumbled. She fell down and hit her head on the table. "Ow!" Lily said. "That hurts!" She started to cry. Ben ran to her and hugged her. He said, "I'm sorry, Lily. Are you okay?" Mom heard the noise and the crying. She woke up and came to the kitchen. She saw the mess and the kids. She said, "What happened here?" Lily and Ben looked at Mom. They said, "We wanted to make waffles for you. But we made a big mess. And Lily got hurt. We're sorry, Mom." Mom smiled and said, "It's okay, sweeties. I appreciate your effort. But next time, please wait for me to help you. Making waffles can be tricky. And dangerous. Come on, let's clean up together. And then we can have some waffles. I have some frozen ones in the freezer. They are easy to heat up. And they are yummy too." Lily and Ben nodded and said, "Okay, Mom. Thank you, Mom. We love you, Mom." Mom hugged them and said, "I love you too, Lily and Ben. You are my brave and kind waffle makers."

The water felt so fast and powerful. "This creek looks dangerous," said Jake. "We should go back to the plain". But Jill didn't take his advice. She waded into the water, eager to make a splash. "Be careful!" Jake called after her. But it was too late - Jill was already swept away by the fast-moving current. Jake was so worried, but he didn't give up. He ran until he found a nearby farmer and sold him a sack of apples from the farm. With the money the farmer gave him, Jake was able to buy a long rope and throw it into the water. Jill managed to grab the rope and was pulled to safety. The moral of this story is to always listen to your friends and never take risks that may be dangerous to your health.

She finds some leaves and mud and sticks them on the hole. She finds a feather and ties it to the mast. She says, "There, now it is a better boat." Ben says, "But it is still broken. It will not go fast. My stone will win the race." He picks up a big stone and throws it in the pond. It makes a huge splash. Lily says, "No, my boat will win. It is not broken, it is special. The boat reaches the other side of the pond. Lily says, "Yay, I win! My boat is the best!" Ben says, "No fair, your boat had a feather. That is cheating." He runs to the other side of the pond and grabs the boat. He breaks the feather and throws the boat back in the water. Lily says, "Hey, stop that! You are mean! You broke my boat!" They put their sandwiches, apples, and cookies in the cart and pushed it along the road. On the way, they saw a big pile of dirt. Lily wanted to stop and play with the dirt. She said, "Let's bury our cart in the dirt. It will be fun!" Ben did not think it was a good idea. He said, "No, Lily. That is not fun. That is silly. We need our cart for our picnic. And we have to be careful. The dirt might have bugs or worms or rocks." But Lily did not listen to Ben. She was independent and wanted to do what she wanted. She said, "I don't care. I want to bury our cart. You can go to the park by yourself. I don't need you." She grabbed the cart and pulled it to the pile of dirt. Ben was sad and angry. He did not want to lose his friend or his cart. He tried to stop Lily, but she was too strong. She pushed him away and dumped the cart in the dirt. Then she started to shovel the dirt over the cart with her hands. She laughed and said, "Look, Ben. I buried our cart. Isn't it funny?" Ben did not think it was funny. He thought it was mean and stupid. He said, "No, Lily. It is not funny. It is bad. You ruined our cart and our picnic. And you hurt my feelings. You are not a good friend." She wished she had not been independent and selfish. She learned a lesson. She learned that burying her cart was not fun. It was foolish. She learned that being independent was not good. It was lonely. She learned that being a friend was better. It was happy. The end.

Mia and Tim learned that it's good to be curious and learn about the universe, but they should not take risks without thinking. They went back inside and continued to look at the book, asking Mia's mom many questions about the stars and the universe.

"Yes, I can!" Ben said. He took off his shoes and socks and ran to the pond. He jumped in the water and tried to grab the fish. But the water was cold and deep. Ben felt scared and cold. He could not breathe. He could not see the fish. "Help, Anna, help!" Ben cried. He splashed and kicked, but he could not get out. Anna heard Ben's cry. She ran to the pond. She saw Ben in the water. She was scared, too. But she saw something else. She saw a big ball of wool on the grass. It was soft and warm. It was from her grandma's sweater. She had dropped it when she ran to the pond. Anna had an idea. She grabbed the wool and threw it to Ben. "Catch, Ben, catch!" she shouted. "It is wool. It will help you." Ben saw the wool. He reached for it and caught it. He felt the wool in his hands. It was soft and warm. It made him feel better. He wrapped the wool around his arms and chest. He pulled the wool and moved closer to the shore. Anna helped him out of the water. "Are you okay, Ben?" Anna asked. She hugged him and rubbed his back. "I'm okay, Anna," Ben said. He shivered and coughed. "Thank you for the wool. It was cool. It saved me." "You're welcome, Ben," Anna said. She smiled and kissed his cheek. "But next time, don't dive in the pond. It is not safe. And it is not fun." "Okay, Anna," Ben said. He nodded and smiled. "Next time, I will feed the ducks with you. And we will use the wool to make them a nest."

Don't go too far," mom says. Ben and Lily enter the tent. It is dark and cool. They see many things. They see a ball, a doll, a car and a hat. They play with the things. They are happy. "Look, I found a shirt!" Lily says. She holds a green shirt. It has a picture of a frog. "It is funny!" Ben says. He laughs. They put on the shirt. They pretend to be frogs. They hop and croak. They have fun. Mom hears them. She smiles. She is happy too. She enters the tent. She joins them. "Ribbit, ribbit!" mom says. "Ribbit, ribbit!" Ben and Lily say. They hug mom. They are a happy frog family.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toy soldiers. He would pretend the soldiers were in a war. Tim was a lucky boy because he had many friends to play with. One sunny day, Tim went to the park. He saw a girl playing with her dolls. Her name was Lily. Tim wanted to meet her, so he walked up to her and said, "Hi, I'm Tim. Do you want to play with me?" Lily smiled and said, "Sure, Tim! Let's play together." They decided to play war with Tim's toy soldiers and Lily's dolls. They had lots of fun, and their toys became friends too. As the sun began to set, Tim and Lily knew it was time to go home. They said goodbye and promised to meet again at the park. Tim felt lucky to have a new friend, and he couldn't wait to play with Lily again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat sugar. One day, she found a big bag of sugar in the kitchen. She took the bag and hid it in her room. Later that day, Lily's old grandpa came to visit. He saw the sugar bag in her room and said, "Lily, eating too much sugar is not good for you. It can make you sick." But Lily didn't listen to her grandpa. She ate the sugar when no one was looking. That night, Lily had a dream. In her dream, a big, scary monster came to her room. The monster said, "I am the Sugar Monster! I will scare you every night if you keep eating too much sugar!" Lily woke up, scared and crying. She ran to her grandpa and told him about the dream. Grandpa hugged her and said, "Don't worry, Lily. If you stop eating too much sugar, the Sugar Monster won't scare you anymore." Lily learned her lesson and stopped eating too much sugar. She shared the sugar with her friends and family, and they all enjoyed it together. The Sugar Monster never scared her again. The moral of the story is to listen to the advice of our elders and not to overindulge in things that can harm us.

Sarah was so excited about it that she ran straight away to do it! On her way to the bedroom, she saw her younger brother waving at her from the living room. He asked her if he could help her, but Sarah quickly said "No way!" She wanted to be the one to complete the task. After she made it to the bedroom, she proudly announced, "I did it! I carried the weight!" Her parents praised her and said, "You are so dependable. Great job, Sarah!" She smiled and felt proud of herself. Everyone was so excited that day that it was the perfect way to end their day. Sarah said, "Let's play hide and seek now!" So they spent the rest of the day playing, having fun and enjoying the sweetness of that wonderful moment.

Lily smiled and said, "Yes, Zigzag! Come sit with me." They both sat under the tree, feeling the cool shade. They talked and laughed, becoming good friends. After a while, Zigzag and Lily saw another dog running around without resting. The dog looked very tired but didn't stop to rest. Zigzag said to Lily, "We should tell him to rest, so he doesn't get too tired." Lily agreed and they both called the dog over. They told him the importance of resting when feeling tired. The dog listened and decided to rest with them. They all sat under the tree, happy and resting. The moral of the story is to always rest when you feel tired and to help others do the same.

"I found it and thought it would be fun to play with," said Bobo. Riri wanted to play too, but the pole was too big for her. Bobo felt uncomfortable because the pole was too big for Riri to play with. Bobo thought of an idea. He broke the pole into two smaller poles. Now, Riri could play with one and Bobo could play with the other. They played together all day and had lots of fun. In the end, they learned that sharing makes everyone happy.

Tim and Jen like to play with cars. They have many cars of different colors and sizes. They make roads with blocks and tape. They zoom their cars on the roads and make noises. One day, Tim and Jen have a problem. They both want to use the same car. It is a big red car with shiny wheels. It is their favorite car. They both grab the car and pull hard. "Mine!" Tim says. "No, mine!" Jen says. They pull and pull and pull. The car does not like it. It makes a loud crack. The car breaks in two. Tim and Jen are sad. They look at the broken car. They hear a voice. It is Mom. She comes into the room and sees the mess. She is not happy. "What happened here?" Mom asks. "Tim and Jen broke the car!" they both say. Mom shakes her head. She picks up the pieces of the car. She looks at them. "This was a cheap car," Mom says. "It was not made well. It was not strong. It could not last in this world." She puts the pieces in the trash. She looks at Tim and Jen. "Next time, you need to share," Mom says. "You need to take turns. You need to yield. Do you know what yield means?" Tim and Jen shake their heads. "Yield means to let someone else go first," Mom says. "It means to be kind and fair. It means to not be greedy and selfish. Can you do that?" Tim and Jen nod. "Yes, Mom," they say. Mom smiles. She hugs them. "Good," she says. "Now, let's find another car to play with. A better car. A car that can yield."

Tom and Sam liked to play golf with their dad. They had a big bag of balls and sticks. They went to a field near their house. Their dad showed them how to hit the balls far and high. Tom and Sam tried to do the same, but they were not very good. Sometimes they missed the ball, sometimes they hit it too hard or too soft. "Can I have more balls, dad?" Tom asked. He had lost many balls in the grass or the trees. "Sure, son, you can have more balls. But be careful, don't hit them too close to the road. There are cars and people there. You don't want to hurt anyone or break anything," their dad said. He was a generous dad. He gave Tom and Sam many balls and sticks. Tom nodded and took some more balls. He wanted to hit them as far as his dad. He saw a big hill on the other side of the field. He thought it would be fun to hit the balls over the hill. He did not know what was behind the hill. He swung his stick hard and hit a ball. The ball flew over the hill. He did not see where it landed. He smiled and hit another ball. And another. And another. Sam watched Tom and wondered what was behind the hill. He decided to follow one of the balls. He ran after it and climbed the hill. He was surprised by what he saw. Behind the hill was a big house. It had many windows and a nice garden. It also had many holes and cracks. Some of the windows were broken. Some of the flowers were smashed. Some of the balls were stuck in the walls or the roof. Sam heard a loud voice. It was a man. He was angry. He came out of the house. He had a ball in his hand. He threw it at Sam. "Hey, you! Stop destroying my house! Who are you? Where is your dad? I'm going to call the police!" the man shouted. Sam was scared. He did not know what to do. He ran back to the hill. He called Tom and their dad. "Dad, dad, come quick! There is a man behind the hill. He is mad. He says we destroyed his house. He has some of our balls. He wants to call the police!" Sam said. Their dad was shocked. He dropped his stick and ran to the hill. He saw the man and the house. He saw the damage. He felt sorry and ashamed. He apologized to the man. He explained that he did not know there was a house behind the hill. He said that his sons were just playing golf. He said that he would pay for the repairs. He asked the man not to call the police. The man was still angry, but he calmed down a bit. He saw that their dad was sincere and sorry. He saw that Tom and Sam were young and did not mean to destroy his house. He decided to be generous and forgive them. He accepted their dad's apology and offer. He gave back their balls. He told them to be more careful next time. He told them to go home and not come back. Their dad thanked the man and took Tom and Sam by the hand. He walked them back to their house. He was not happy. He scolded them for being careless and reckless. He told them that they had to learn to respect other people's property and safety. He told them that they could not play golf anymore until they were older and wiser. Tom and Sam felt bad. They knew they had done wrong. They said they were sorry to their dad and the man. They promised to be more careful and respectful. They hoped that the man's house would be fixed soon. They hoped that he would not be mad at them forever. They learned a hard lesson that day. They learned that playing golf can be fun, but also dangerous. They learned that destroying things can have bad consequences. They learned that being generous can make things better.

One day, a little bug named Billy was crawling on the ground. He saw an interesting pill on the floor. Billy was curious and wanted to know what it was. He looked at the pill and thought, "I have never seen this before!" Billy crawled over to his friend, Sally the snail. He asked her, "Sally, do you know what this interesting pill is?" Sally looked at the pill and said, "No, Billy, I don't know. But we should be careful with it." Billy and Sally decided to take the pill to their friend, Tim the turtle. As they crawled to Tim's house, they talked about the interesting pill. When they got there, Tim was very excited to see them. "Hi, Billy and Sally!" he said. Billy showed Tim the interesting pill and asked, "Tim, do you know what this is?" Tim looked at the pill and said, "I don't know either, but it's very interesting. We should ask our big friend, Mr. Owl. He knows many things." So, Billy, Sally, and Tim crawled to Mr. Owl's tree. They showed Mr. Owl the interesting pill. Mr. Owl smiled and said, "This is a pill that helps people feel better when they are sick. It's not for us, but it's good to know what it is." Billy, Sally, and Tim thanked Mr. Owl and crawled back home, happy to have learned something new.

But then, something unexpected happened. The bird ate the berry and started to reverse! It flew backward! Mia and her mom were very surprised. They looked at each other and laughed. "Wow," said Mia's mom, "I guess those wild berries are magic berries!" They watched the bird fly backward until it was out of sight. Mia knew she made the right choice by listening to her mom.

The box was in a nice, warm place. The bird thought, "This is a good place to settle and make my home." The gray bird moved into the cardboard box. It made a cozy nest inside. The bird was very happy and safe in its new home. Every day, the bird sang a happy song. And that's how the little gray bird found a home and settled in the big cardboard box.

Soon, the ball went near a dry tree. Tim wanted to stop the ball before it hit the tree. He ran fast to catch the ball. But, the ball was too quick for him. It hit the tree and made a loud noise. Suddenly, the dry tree started to shake. Tim was scared and stopped running. But then, to his surprise, the tree was not dry anymore. It became a big, green tree with lots of leaves. The ball had magic in it! Tim was happy and played with the magic ball all day.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby fish named Bob. Bob lived in a big tank with his friends. They liked to play and swim all day. One day, Bob saw a new toy in the tank. It was a hoop. Bob had an idea. Bob said to his friends, "Let's test who can swim through the hoop the fastest!" All the fish were excited and wanted to try. They lined up and took turns swimming through the hoop. Some were fast, and some were slow. At last, it was Bob's turn. Everyone thought the chubby fish would be slow. But Bob had a secret: he was the best swimmer! He swam through the hoop very fast. All his friends were surprised. They cheered for Bob and learned that even a chubby fish can be a fast swimmer.

Lucy was very surprised that the dog could talk! She said, "Hi Buddy! I want to learn here." Buddy told her, "I will show you around." Lucy and her mom followed Buddy. They saw lots of big rooms and many people. But then, something unexpected happened. Buddy turned into a big, wise teacher! He said, "Now I can teach you here at the big place." Lucy was very happy. She learned a lot from Buddy the teacher. She knew that the big place was a special place where she could learn and grow. Her mom was proud of her and they had a great day at the big place called "university".

It was a small animal! John looked closer and saw that it was a rabbit! He was so excited. He wanted to pet the rabbit and make it his new friend. But the rabbit was very scared and ran away. John tried again and again, but the rabbit refused to come close. That was the end of his adventure. He went back inside, still a bit sad. He had wanted to have some fun but it didn't work out. "Look, Ben, there are apples and pears and plums. Let's get some," Lila said. "OK, Lila, but be careful. The tree is very high and the fruits are very far," Ben said. They found a long stick and tried to reach the fruits. Lila held the stick and Ben helped her. They managed to get some apples and pears, but the plums were too high. "I want a plum, Ben. They are purple and sweet. Can you get me one?" Lila asked. "Sure, Lila, I will try. You are my friend and I am dependable. Hold the stick for me," Ben said. He climbed on the stick and reached for a plum. But the stick was not strong enough. It broke and Ben fell down. He hit his head on a rock and started to bleed. "Ben, Ben, are you OK? Wake up, Ben!" Lila cried. She ran to him and tried to wipe the blood from his face. But it was too much. She was scared and sad. She did not know what to do. "Help, help, someone help us!" she shouted. But no one heard her. They were alone in the park. The fruits were forgotten. Ben did not wake up. Lila cried and cried.

The old man would give them some coins for their things, and they would use them to buy candy or toys. One day, they found a big copper pot in the trash. It was very heavy and dirty, but they were happy because they thought it was worth a lot of coins. They dragged it to the old man's shop, hoping he would be nice and give them a good price. But when they got there, they saw that the old man was not there. Instead, there was a new man, who had a harsh face and a mean voice. He looked at the pot and said, "This is junk. I'll give you one coin for it." Ben and Tom were shocked and sad. They knew the pot was worth more than that. They said, "No, thank you. We'll take it somewhere else." The new man got angry and said, "No, you won't. You'll give me the pot, or I'll call the police and tell them you stole it from me." Ben and Tom were scared and didn't know what to do. They wished the old man was there to help them. They looked around and saw a sign that said, "Back in 10 minutes." They hoped the old man would come back soon and save them from the new man.

He was very curious and he knew there was something special inside the tree. So later that night, when he thought his mother wasn't paying attention, he sneaked back to the tree. And sure enough, there was something special inside - a big family of squirrels living in the top of the tree. Buzz was so excited by all the activities and noises the squirrels were making. He knew he had found something truly special. His mother was a bit angry when she found out, but she was also proud of Buzz for being so curious. Max was a serious dog and always ready to help. Max said, "Yes, Tom! Let's clean the mattress so you can sleep on it." Together, Tom and Max took the dirty mattress to the water. They splashed water on it and scrubbed it until it was clean. When the mattress was clean, Tom and Max put it in the sun to dry. Tom and Max were happy that they cleaned the mattress. Now, Tom could sleep on it. Tom said, "Thank you, Max, for helping me. Now we can both sleep on the clean mattress." Max smiled and said, "You're welcome, Tom. I'm always here to help you." That night, Tom and Max slept on the clean mattress. They were happy and had sweet dreams. And they knew that they could always count on each other to help when they needed it.

They pushed their cars as fast as they could. But then, something unexpected happened. Tim's car hit a big rock and break into pieces. "Oh no! My car is broken!" Tim cried. Sam felt bad for Tim. One day, when they zoom to the zoo, they see a sign that says "Seal Show". They are very happy and want to see the seal do tricks. They zoom to the gate and push it open. They zoom inside and see the seal. He is resting on a big rock. He sees them and says "arf arf". Zara and Zane are very happy and say "hello seal". They zoom closer to him and want to touch him. But they don't know that the gate is for the people who work at the zoo and take care of the seal. They don't know that the seal is not used to strangers and might get scared or angry. They don't know that they are in trouble. Soon, they hear a loud voice that says "Hey, what are you doing here? This is not for visitors. Get out of here, now!" They turn around and see a man in a blue shirt. He is the seal keeper. He is not happy. He is very angry. He runs to them and grabs their bikes. He says "You are not supposed to be here. You could have hurt the seal or yourself. You are very naughty. I'm going to tell your parents." Zara and Zane are very scared and sorry. They say "We're sorry, we just wanted to see the seal. We like the seal. Please don't be mad. Please don't tell our parents." They start to cry. The seal keeper sees that they are just kids who made a mistake. He is still angry, but he is also kind. He says "OK, OK, calm down. I won't tell your parents, but you have to promise me that you will never do this again. You have to respect the animals and the rules. Do you understand?" Zara and Zane nod and say "Yes, we understand. We promise. We're sorry." The seal keeper gives them their bikes and says "Now, go back to the main gate and wait for me there. I'll take you to the seal show and show you how to watch the seal safely and properly. But don't ever zoom in here again. Deal?" Zara and Zane say "Deal. Thank you. We're sorry." They zoom out of the gate and wait for the seal keeper. They feel bad for what they did, but they also feel glad that they can still see the seal. They hope the seal is not mad at them. They hope he will do tricks for them. They hope he will say "arf arf" again. They love the seal.

She was not happy. "Lily, you destroyed my new makeup!" she said. Lily felt bad and said, "I'm sorry, Mom. I will help you clean it up." From that day on, Lily learned to ask before using things that were not hers. She played with her toys and left her mom's makeup alone. And they lived happily ever after.

We must care for it." Tim understood. He played with his friends away from the tree. They still had fun. The moral value of the story is to respect and care for old things, like the ancient tree, and to find other ways to have fun without causing harm.

Jack begged and pleaded until finally, Grandma allowed him to go on it. Jack rushed up the logs and started playing, but soon the logs started to creak and crack even more than before. Grandma looked worried, so Jack quickly jumped off and ran back to her. Grandma hugged Jack and said, "You have to listen to your elders, Jack! This structure wouldn’t have been safe for you to play on. Next time, make sure to follow my instructions.” Moral of the story: Always listen to your elders, because they are wiser and know what is best for you.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who had a big dream. She wanted to go to the moon. But she could not fly, so she decided to build a rocket. One day her Grandma took her to the workshop. She showed the little girl all the different tools. Then the Grandma said, "Now, let's build the rocket!" The little girl and the Grandma worked together to build the rocket. They put on the steel, with bolts and screws. Then the Grandma said, "Let's seat it with cream and make it warm." So they put cream on the rocket and made it warm with a heater. The little girl was so happy with their work! She said, "We just have to push the red button to launch the rocket!" But, Grandma said, "Let's wait until the morning. We will launch the rocket tomorrow, when the sun is warm!" The little girl smiled and went off to bed, dreaming of her big adventure. The end.

Lily played with her ball while mom and dad had a picnic. The loyal vehicle watched and waited. When it was time to go home, the family got back in the car. The car was happy to be with the family and take them home safely. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a girl named Lily went to the park. She saw a big tree with a small door on it. Lily was very curious, so she opened the door. Inside, she found a little room with a table. On the table, there was a jug and a note. Lily kneeled down to read the note. It said, "Drink from the jug if you want something spicy." She wanted to try it, so she drank from the jug. The drink was very spicy, and it made her mouth feel hot. Lily did not like it, so she put the jug back on the table. Then, something unexpected happened. Lily felt her body get smaller and smaller. She looked at her hands and saw that they were tiny. Lily had turned into a little bug! She was very scared and did not know what to do. But then, she saw another note on the table. It said, "Drink again to go back." So Lily drank from the jug one more time, and she turned back into a girl. She was very happy and ran out of the tree, promising never to drink from a strange jug again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a pretty watch that her mommy gave her. Lily wore her watch every day. She loved to look at the time and show her friends her pretty watch. One day, Lily was playing in the park. She saw a boy with an empty ice cream cone. The boy was sad, and his face was dirty. Lily walked up to the boy and said, "Hi, I am Lily. Why are you sad?" The boy looked at Lily and said, "I dropped my ice cream, and now my cone is empty." Lily wanted to help the boy feel better. She took a napkin from her pocket and wiped the boy's face clean. "It's okay," she said. "We can get more ice cream." Lily and the boy went to the ice cream truck and got new ice cream cones. They sat on a bench, enjoying their ice cream and talking. Lily showed the boy her pretty watch, and they became good friends. From that day on, Lily and the boy played together in the park every day.

Once upon a time, there was a silly cat named Tom. Tom liked to play with his red ball all day. One day, Tom saw a big pan in the kitchen. He thought it would be fun to play with the pan. Tom jumped into the pan and tried to squeeze the ball. "Squeeze, squeeze," he said, laughing. But the ball was too big for the pan. Tom's friend, a little dog named Max, came into the kitchen. Max saw Tom in the pan and said, "Tom, you are so silly! You can't play with the pan." But Tom didn't listen. He tried to squeeze the ball harder. Suddenly, the pan tipped over and Tom fell out. He bumped his head and started to cry. Max tried to help Tom, but it was too late. Tom had learned his lesson not to play with things that were not toys.

"What kind of things do you need?" asked Sofia. "I have lots of things like food, clothes and toys," said the man. Sofia was very excited. She wanted to help the man. "I will help you supply things!" she said. So Sofia jogged around the village helping the man supply all the people she knew. When the mission was finished, Sofia gave the man a big hug. Ben and Lily liked to play in the kitchen. They liked to mix things in bowls and pretend they were chefs. One day, they found a big jar of season on the shelf. It had many colors and smells inside. "Let's mix some season with our soup!" Ben said. "OK!" Lily agreed. They poured some season into their bowls and stirred with spoons. "Yum, this smells good!" Ben said. "Can we taste it?" Lily asked. "Sure, why not?" Ben said. He took a sip of his soup and made a funny face. "Blech! This is yucky!" Lily tried her soup and spat it out. "Ew! This is spicy!" They heard their mom coming into the kitchen. They quickly hid their bowls under the table. "Hi, kids! What are you doing?" Mom asked. "We are playing chefs!" Ben said. "That's nice. Do you want to help me make some real soup?" Mom asked. "Yes, please!" Ben and Lily said. They were polite and wanted to learn from Mom. Mom showed them how to chop some vegetables and add some water and salt. She also took the jar of season from the shelf. "This is not for soup, kids. This is for curry. It has many spices that make your mouth burn. You have to be careful with it." Mom said. "Oh, we didn't know that." Ben and Lily said. "It's OK. You were curious and creative. But next time, ask me before you use something you don't know." Mom said. "OK, Mom. We're sorry." Ben and Lily said. Mom smiled and hugged them. "It's OK. I love you. Now, let's enjoy our soup together." She said. They all sat down and ate their soup. It was warm and tasty. Ben and Lily learned a lesson and had fun with Mom. They decided to save the season for another day.

After a long time, the ant reached the top. He looked around and saw his home. He was happy. The humble ant had done it. He climbed back down and told his family about the top of the tree. They were proud of him.

Jump up and move your wings like this. So the little bird began to dance, moving her wings and legs up and down. Suddenly, the fairy began to laugh and said, "Look, you can fly! Now go and get some juice to celebrate!" The little bird was so happy and flew off in search of some juice. She soon spotted a nearby tree with lots of juicy fruit and flew over. She picked the juiciest one and flew back to her friends who were all excited to celebrate with her. And they all drank the juice and danced together, celebrating the poor little bird's newfound abilities!

The mattress could spring up and down, and Lily loved to jump on it. "Hi, Lily!" said the mattress. "Want to play with me?" Lily was very happy and said, "Yes! Let's play!" They played all day, jumping up and down. But the mattress told Lily, "If you jump too much, you might fall and get hurt. So be careful!" Lily nodded and kept playing, but she remembered what the mattress said. One day, Lily's friend, Tim, came over to play. They both jumped on the mattress, having lots of fun. But Tim didn't listen when Lily told him to be careful. He jumped too high and fell off the mattress. He got hurt and cried. Lily helped him up and said, "You should have listened. The mattress told me to be careful, and I didn't get hurt." From that day, Tim learned to listen to others and be careful while playing. And the weird, talking mattress became a good friend to both Lily and Tim, always reminding them to play safe.

"OK, Ben, I will help you," Lily said. They raked and raked until they had a lot of leaves. They made a big wall with the leaves around a tree. They crawled inside and pretended it was their castle. "This is our castle, and we are the king and the queen," Ben said. "Yes, and we have a lot of food in our castle," Lily said. She picked up a leaf and pretended it was a cookie. She offered it to Ben. "Here, Ben, eat this cookie. It is very yummy," she said. Ben looked at the leaf. He knew it was not a real cookie. It was a fake cookie. He did not want to eat it. "No, thank you, Lily. I do not want to eat the leaf. It is not a cookie. It is fake," he said. Lily frowned. She did not like that Ben did not want to play with her. "But it is fun to pretend, Ben. We can pretend anything we want in our castle," she said. Ben thought for a moment. He did not want to make Lily sad. He decided to play along. "OK, Lily, I will eat the cookie. Mmm, it is very yummy," he said. He pretended to eat the leaf. Lily smiled. She was happy that Ben played with her. They ate more fake cookies and had a lot of fun in their castle. They did not notice that their mom was watching them from the window. She smiled too. She was proud of her children. They had big imaginations and kind hearts.

Ben and Lily were having breakfast with their mom and dad. They liked to eat toast and eggs and juice. But today, mom had a surprise for them. She put a big plate of sausages on the table. "Look, kids, I made some sausages for you. They are very yummy and good for you. Do you want to try some?" mom asked. Ben's eyes lit up. He loved sausages. He reached for the plate and took the biggest sausage he could find. It was enormous. He bit into it and smiled. "Mmm, this is delicious, mom. Thank you!" he said. Lily looked at the plate and frowned. She did not like sausages. She thought they were gross and smelly. She pushed her plate away and shook her head. "No, thank you, mom. I don't want any sausages. I only want toast and eggs and juice," she said. Mom and dad looked at each other. They knew that Ben and Lily sometimes disagreed about food. They wanted them to eat a balanced diet and try new things. "Lily, why don't you give the sausages a chance? They are not gross or smelly. They are made of meat and spices and herbs. They have a lot of protein and iron. They will make you strong and healthy," dad said. Lily still did not want to try the sausages. She crossed her arms and pouted. "No, dad. I don't like meat. I don't like spices. I don't like herbs. I only like toast and eggs and juice. Sausages are yucky," she said. Ben looked at his sister and rolled his eyes. He thought she was being silly and stubborn. He wanted her to taste the sausages and see how good they were. "Lily, you are missing out. Sausages are not yucky. They are tasty and juicy and fun. You can dip them in ketchup or mustard or honey. You can make funny faces with them. You can pretend they are worms or snakes or rockets. Come on, just try one bite. Please?" he said. Lily looked at her brother and sighed. She saw that he was enjoying his sausage and that he wanted to share it with her. She decided to be brave and polite. She took a small sausage from the plate and nibbled on it. She was surprised. It was not as bad as she thought. It was actually kind of good. "Hey, this is not so bad. It is kind of good. Thank you, Ben. Thank you, mom and dad. I'm sorry I disagreed with you. I guess I can try new things sometimes," she said. Mom and dad smiled. They were proud of Lily for trying something new. They were also proud of Ben for being kind and helpful. They hugged their kids and gave them more toast and eggs and juice. They all ate their breakfast happily. They agreed that sausages were a good surprise.

One day, a little girl named Suzy went to a big building with her mom. She saw a big box with doors. Her mom said, "That's an elevator, Suzy." Suzy wanted to go inside the elevator, but her mom said it was difficult to use. Suzy's mom went to talk to a man. Suzy saw the elevator doors open. She wanted to peek inside. Suzy went in the elevator and the doors closed. She pushed some buttons and the elevator went up and down. Suzy was scared. A man found Suzy in the elevator. He said, "Are you lost?" Suzy cried and said, "I want my mom." The man tried to help Suzy find her mom, but they couldn't find her. Suzy felt sad because she didn't listen to her mom.

Ben and Lily were playing in the park. They liked to draw with pencils on the ground. Ben had a blue pencil and Lily had a brown pencil. They drew flowers, trees, and animals. "Look, I drew a big bear!" Lily said, showing Ben her drawing. "Wow, that's good!" Ben said. "I drew a lion. He is the king of the jungle." They were having fun, until they saw a man coming. He was wearing a green hat and a green coat. He had a big stick in his hand. He looked angry. "Hey, you kids! What are you doing here? This is my park!" the man shouted. "You are making a mess with your pencils. Go away, or I will make you go away!" Ben and Lily were scared. They grabbed their pencils and ran. But the man was faster. He caught up with them and snatched their pencils. He broke them in half and threw them away. "Ha! That will teach you a lesson!" the man said. "Now, march! March out of my park and don't come back!" Ben and Lily marched away, crying. They had lost their pencils and their drawings. They felt very sad and angry. They wished they had a big bear or a lion to help them. But they had nothing. They had no friends in the park.

Lily and Ben are twins. They like to play in their room. But sometimes they want to go outside and see the flowers and the birds. One day, they have a plan. They wait until Mom is busy in the kitchen. Then they sneak out of their room. They are very quiet. They do not want Mom to hear them. They go to the back door. It is locked. They look for the key. They find it on a hook. Ben reaches for it. He is taller than Lily. He gets the key and puts it in the lock. He turns it. The door opens. They smile at each other. They escape. They run to the garden. They see many colors and shapes. They smell the sweet air. They hear the chirping and the buzzing. They feel the sun and the breeze. They are happy. They pick some flowers. They make a crown for Lily and a necklace for Ben. They pretend they are king and queen of the garden. But then they hear a loud voice. It is Mom. She is angry. She sees them in the garden. She runs to them. She grabs them by their arms. She pulls them back to the house. She scolds them. She says they are naughty. They should not go outside without her. They could get hurt or lost. She says they have to stay in their room. She says they have to be quiet. She says they have to be tight. She means they have to hug each other and say sorry. Lily and Ben are sad. They do not like to be scolded. They do not like to be in their room. They do not like to be quiet. They do not like to be tight. They want to be free. They want to be in the garden. They want to escape. But they know they cannot. They hug each other and say sorry. They hope Mom will forgive them. They hope they can go outside again. They hope they can play in their room. They hope they can have fun. They hope they can be happy.

Bob ran as fast as he could to his house. He shouted to his friends, "Help me! A big bird wants to eat me!" His friends came out and made loud noises to scare the bird away. The bird was scared of the loud noises and flew away. Bob was safe now. He thanked his friends and said, "You saved me! I am so happy to have such good friends." From that day on, Bob and his friends played together every day. They were always careful and looked out for each other. And they all lived happily ever after.

Jack felt a little dizzy. He stopped for a rest and found a beautiful leaf. He picked it up and smiled. He looked around and noticed a tree. He wanted to climb it and see the view. He started to climb up the branches but began to feel dizzy. Jack: "Help!" Suddenly, a friendly bird flew down from the sky and said, Bird: "What are you doing little one?" Jack: "I'm trying to climb the tree but I feel dizzy." Bird: "Don't worry, I can help you. Take my wing and I will fly you to the top of the tree." Jack grabbed the bird's wing and soon they were at the top. The view was stunning. Jack smiled and thanked the bird. He waved goodbye and climbed down the tree. Jack was very proud of himself. He had an amazing adventure and learned that it's okay to ask for help when you feel dizzy!

Sam tried very hard, but Fred was faster. Fred laughed and said, "You're too slow, Sam!" Later, Sam and his friends were hungry. They found some spicy fish to eat. Sam took a bite and said, "Wow, this is spicy!" Fred swam by and saw the spicy fish. He wanted to try it too. "Give me some," he said. Sam shared the spicy fish with Fred. Fred took a big bite of the spicy fish. His face turned red, and he started to cough. "This is too spicy for me!" he cried. Sam and his friends laughed. They didn't tease Fred, but they knew that everyone was good at different things. From that day on, Fred didn't tease Sam anymore, and they all played happily together in the sea.

"I did it!" he said with a big smile. But when he looked at the pie, he saw something strange. There were green things on top of it. He thought, "Maybe it will taste good." Tim took a big bite of the pie. "Yuck!" he said. "This pie is disgusting!" His mom came into the room and laughed. "That's because it's a spinach pie, Tim. I made it for dinner tonight." She downed her sweater to make herself look more polite, and slowly went to answer the door. When the door opened, two strange men were standing there. Anna's heart started to beat faster, and she began to tremble again. "Hello," one of the men said politely, "We're here to take your doll." Instinctively, Anna hugged her doll tightly and tried to close the door. However, the men were too fast and hindered her. Kicking the door open, they snatched the doll away from her. Anna started to cry. With the doll gone and the men walking away, Anna realized she had made a big mistake. The men had looked and acted like they didn't belong there, and she should have known to stay away from them. Tom and Mia liked to play with magnets. They had many magnets of different shapes and colors. They stuck them on the fridge, on the door, on the table. They made pictures and patterns with them. One day, they found a big magnet in the garage. It was silver and round and very strong. Tom and Mia wanted to try it. They took it to the kitchen and stuck it on the fridge. It made a loud clack sound. "Wow, this magnet is so interesting!" Mia said. "Look, it can hold many other magnets on it!" "Let me try!" Tom said. He took some magnets from the fridge and put them on the big magnet. They also made a loud clack sound and stayed on the big magnet. Tom and Mia had fun with the big magnet. They forgot about their mom, who was making lunch. She had a note on the fridge that said: "Don't forget to wash your hands before eating." The note had a small magnet on it. But when Tom and Mia stuck the big magnet on the fridge, the note fell off. The small magnet was not strong enough to stay on the fridge with the big magnet. The note landed on the floor, under the table. Tom and Mia did not see the note. They did not wash their hands. They played with the big magnet until mom called them for lunch. "Come on, kids, it's time to eat!" mom said. "Did you wash your hands?" Tom and Mia looked at each other. They did not remember to wash their hands. They remembered the big magnet. "Sorry, mom, we forgot," Tom said. "Why did you forget?" mom asked. "Because we found a big magnet in the garage," Mia said. "It was so interesting!" Mom looked at the fridge. She saw the big magnet and the other magnets on it. She did not see the note. "Where is the note I left you?" mom asked. Tom and Mia looked at the fridge. They did not see the note. They wondered where it was. Mom looked under the table. She saw the note on the floor. She picked it up and showed it to Tom and Mia. "Here is the note," mom said. "It says: Don't forget to wash your hands before eating. The big magnet made the note fall off. The big magnet did not remind you to wash your hands. It made you forget." Tom and Mia felt sorry. They said sorry to mom. They went to the bathroom and washed their hands. They learned that magnets are interesting, but they are not good at reminding.

A little rabbit saw the tears on the bear's face. “Why are you crying?” said the rabbit. The bear sniffed and said “I am scared of the rain - I don't understand it.” The rabbit smiled and said “It is okay, it's only water falling from the sky. Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved to watch videos on his little TV. One day, he found a video about a farm. He watched the video and saw a big tractor. The tractor was helping the farmer yield many fruits and vegetables. Tim thought the tractor was very cool. Tim wanted to help his mom in their garden, just like the tractor in the video. He put on his hat and went outside. The sun was harsh, but Tim didn't care. He wanted to be a helper like the tractor. He started to dig and plant seeds in the ground. "Mom, look! I am helping like the tractor!" Tim said proudly. His mom smiled and said, "Good job, Tim! You are a great helper!" Tim felt happy that he could help his mom. They worked together in the garden all day, just like the tractor and the farmer in the video.

Once upon a time, there was a stubborn man. He loved to carry big bags. One day, he wanted to go on a walk with his big bag. He felt very strong. While walking, he saw a big rock. He wanted to pick it up and put it in his bag. But the rock was too heavy. He tried and tried, but he could not lift it. He felt sad. Then, a little boy came to help. The little boy said, "Drop some things from your bag, and then you can pick up the rock." The stubborn man listened to the little boy. He dropped some things from his bag, and then he could pick up the rock. They both felt happy and walked home together.

Jessie was riding her bike with her dad. She had a leather bag attached to her bike handle. She was so happy and feeling so strong. But then, suddenly, something went wrong. They heard a loud noise from the bike. “Oh, no!” her dad said. “We need to remove the bag, Jessie.” So, dad and Jessie carefully removed the bag. When they opened it, a big, black snake came out! Dad quickly ran away, but Jessie was too scared to move. The snake was really strong and it bit Jessie’s arm. Tears rolled down her cheeks as she saw the snake slither away, back into the leather bag. Jessie and her dad went to the hospital, where the doctor removed all of the poison from her arm. But, it was too late. Jessie’s arm was badly hurt and she would never be able to play with her bike ever again.

Once upon a time, there was a big museum. It was empty. No one was there. It was quiet. In the museum, there was a big lion. The lion was not real, but it looked real. The lion liked to roar. When it roared, it was loud. One day, a little boy came to the museum. He saw the big lion. The lion roared. The little boy was not scared. He laughed and clapped. The lion and the little boy became friends. They played together in the empty museum. And they were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a gentle cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play with her ball. She would run and jump all day long. Kitty had a best friend named Tim. Tim was a little boy who liked to play with Kitty. They were always happy together. One day, Tim found a small pocket in his pants. He did not know what it was for. He showed it to Kitty and asked, "What can we do with this pocket?" Kitty did not know either, but she wanted to find out. Tim and Kitty went outside to play. They found a small, shiny rock. Tim said, "Let's put the rock in the pocket!" He put the rock in the pocket and it fit just right. Tim and Kitty were very happy. They knew that the pocket was a special place to keep things they find. From that day on, they always played together and found more things to put in the pocket.

Once upon a time, there was a blue toy car. It was very fast and could go zoom, zoom, zoom! The car had a battery inside to help it go. One day, the battery was gone! The car could not go zoom, zoom, zoom anymore. The blue toy car was sad. The blue toy car had a friend, a little girl named Lily. Lily wanted to help her friend, so she started to look for the battery. She looked under the bed, but it was not there. She looked in the toy box, but it was not there either. The battery was hiding very well. Finally, Lily found the battery hiding behind the big chair. She put the battery back inside the blue toy car. The car was so happy! Now, it could go zoom, zoom, zoom again. Lily and the blue toy car played together all day long. They were the best of friends.

Anna was a shy girl who liked to play with her dolls. She had a big sister, Mia, who liked to play with her friends. Mia was not shy at all. She liked to talk and laugh and run. One day, Mia and her friends came to Anna's room. They saw Anna's dolls and wanted to play with them. Mia said, "Anna, can we play with your dolls? They are so pretty and cute." Anna felt scared. She did not want to share her dolls with Mia's friends. She did not know them. She said, "No, these are my dolls. You have your own toys. Go away." Mia was angry. She said, "Anna, you are so mean and selfish. You have to share your toys. We are your guests. You have to be nice to us." Anna said, "No, you are not my guests. You are Mia's guests. This is my room. You have to be nice to me." Mia and her friends did not listen. They grabbed Anna's dolls and started to play with them. They made them talk and dance and sing. They did not care if they broke them or dirtied them. Anna cried. She felt sad and angry. She wanted her dolls back. She said, "Stop it! Stop it! Give me my dolls! You are hurting them!" Mia and her friends laughed. They said, "Anna, you are so silly and babyish. These are just dolls. They are not real. They do not feel anything. They do not need oxygen or food or water. They are just toys." Anna said, "No, they are not just toys. They are my friends. They have names and stories and feelings. They need oxygen and food and water like me. They are real to me." Mia and her friends did not understand. They thought Anna was weird and crazy. They said, "Anna, you are so weird and crazy. You need to grow up and play with real friends. You need to stop being so shy and have fun." Anna said, "No, you are the ones who are weird and crazy. You do not know how to play with dolls. You do not know how to be gentle and kind. You do not know how to have fun." Mia and her friends did not like Anna. They said, "Anna, you are no fun at all. We do not want to play with you. You are not our friend. You are just a baby." Anna said, "I do not want to play with you either. You are not my friends. You are just mean and rude. You are just bullies." Mia and her friends left Anna's room. They took some of her dolls with them. They said, "Bye, Anna. We do not care about you or your dolls. We have better things to do." Anna was left alone in her room. She felt lonely and hurt. She hugged her remaining dolls and said, "I'm sorry, my friends. I love you. You are the best. You include me and make me happy."

Once upon a time, there was an elderly dog named Max. Max loved to play with his ball. One sunny day, Max was playing with his ball in the park. He was very happy. Max saw a little girl named Amy. She was eating an ice cream. Max wanted to say hi, so he ran to Amy. But he did not see the water on the ground. Max slipped and fell. He felt sad. Amy saw Max fall and said, "Are you okay, Max?" Max nodded, and Amy helped him up. She told Max, "Be careful when you run, so you don't slip." Max learned to be more careful when he played. And from that day, Max and Amy became best friends. They always looked out for each other and learned to be careful together.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy bunny that he loved very much. But one day, the bunny got a big hole in it. Tim was very sad. Tim's mom said, "Don't worry, we can fix bunny with an operation." Tim didn't know what an operation was, but he wanted his bunny to be better. So, Tim's mom got a needle with sharp thread to sew the hole. Tim watched as his mom sewed the hole. The bunny couldn't hear them because it was deaf. After the operation, the bunny was all better. Tim was so happy to have his bunny back. They played together and had lots of fun.

Lily and Anna were having a tea party with their dolls. They poured water from a pink pot into tiny cups and pretended it was tea. They gave their dolls names and voices and made them talk to each other. "Hello, Miss Daisy, how are you today?" Lily said in a high-pitched voice, holding her doll with curly hair. "I'm very well, thank you, Miss Rose. You have such a lovely dress," Anna said in a low voice, holding her doll with a blue dress. They giggled and sipped their water. They felt very grown-up and happy. But then, Lily's big brother Tom came into the room. He was six years old and thought he was very smart. He saw the girls playing with their dolls and water and laughed. "What are you doing, you silly girls? That's not tea, that's water. And those are not real dolls, they are just toys. You are so ignorant, you don't know anything," he said in a mean voice. He grabbed the pink pot and poured the water over Lily's head. Lily gasped and felt the water dripping down her face and clothes. She felt very cold and wet and sad. "Tom, stop it! That's not nice, that's mean. You are so rude, you don't care about anyone," Anna said in an angry voice. She stood up and pushed Tom away. She hugged Lily and tried to comfort her. "Are you okay, Lily? Don't listen to him, he's just a bully. You are not ignorant, you are smart and creative. And you are not silly, you are fun and sweet. And we are not playing with water and toys, we are having a tea party with our dolls. And we are having a lot of fun," she said in a kind voice. Lily nodded and smiled. She felt a little better. She wiped her face and hugged Anna back. "Thank you, Anna. You are a good friend. And you are right, we are having a lot of fun. And he is a bully. And he is the ignorant one, he doesn't know how to play or have fun. And he is the silly one, he just embarrassed himself," she said in a brave voice. They looked at Tom, who was standing there with a red face and a wet shirt. He looked very silly and embarrassed. He realized he had made a mistake and felt sorry. "I'm sorry, Lily. I'm sorry, Anna. I was just joking, I didn't mean to hurt you. Can I join your tea party?" he said in a small voice. Lily and Anna looked at each other and nodded. They decided to forgive him and let him play with them. They gave him a doll and a cup and taught him how to have a tea party. He learned how to be nice and have fun. They all became friends and had a lot of fun.
