Tim liked to sit on the roof of the house and look up at the ceiling inside. One sunny day, Tim saw a seat near the window of the house. He wanted to go inside and sit on the seat. But Tim was too shy. He thought, "What if the people in the house do not like me?" Tim was scared, but he wanted to try. Tim flew to the window and went inside the house. He sat on the seat and looked at the ceiling. Suddenly, the people in the house saw Tim. But they were not mad! They were happy to see Tim. They gave Tim a big hug and some food. Tim was so happy that he was not shy anymore. From that day, Tim and the people in the house were good friends.

Tim was very happy. The bird flew up and got the fruit. But the bird did not give the fruit to Tim. The bird ate all the fruit. Tim was sad. He did not get any fruit. The bird flew away. Tim learned not to trust the bird again.

But then, something unexpected happened. The kite got stuck in a tree. Tim frowned because he did not know what to do. Just then, a big bird flew by. The bird saw the kite in the tree. Once upon a time there was a little fish named Fred. Fred liked to dive deep in the ocean. He also liked to eat jellyfish whenever he found them. One day, Fred went on an adventure. He saw a very big rock. He wanted to swim around it and explore. But, the rock was very slippery, so he was afraid to dive too deep. Suddenly, he heard a splash. He saw that it was a jellyfish, who had fallen onto the rock. He swam a little closer and watched as the jellyfish tried to climb back into the water. The jellyfish was struggling. Fred saw that he could help. He swam up to the jellyfish, and using his strong fins, he pushed the jellyfish off the rock. The jellyfish thanked Fred with a big smile before swimming away. Fred learned a valuable lesson that day. He realised that being useful and helping others can make a big difference. He was glad he had been brave enough to dive deep and follow his adventure.

Lily was a young girl who liked to play with her dolls. She had many dolls, but her favorite was Anna. Anna had long hair, a pink dress and shiny shoes. Lily liked to brush Anna's hair and make her dance. One day, Lily and Anna went to the park with Lily's mom. Lily's mom said they could play on the swings, the slide and the sandbox. Lily was very happy and ran to the swings with Anna. She put Anna on the swing next to her and pushed her gently. "Look, mom, Anna is swinging!" Lily shouted. "Very good, Lily, be careful not to drop her," Lily's mom said. Lily nodded and smiled. She swung higher and higher, feeling the wind in her hair. She looked at Anna and saw that she was smiling too. Lily felt very happy. But then, something bad happened. Lily swung too high and lost her grip on Anna. Anna flew out of the swing and landed on the ground. Lily gasped and stopped swinging. She jumped off the swing and ran to Anna. "Anna, Anna, are you okay?" Lily cried. She picked up Anna and hugged her. She saw that Anna's dress was dirty and torn, and her shoes were missing. She also saw that Anna's pants had fallen off. Lily felt very sad and scared. "Mom, mom, help!" Lily called. Lily's mom heard Lily and ran to her. She saw Lily holding Anna and crying. "What happened, Lily?" Lily's mom asked. "I dropped Anna, mom, I dropped her and she got hurt and her pants fell off," Lily sobbed. Lily's mom hugged Lily and Anna and said, "It's okay, Lily, it's okay. Anna is not hurt, she is just a doll. We can wash her dress and find her shoes and put her pants back on. She will be fine, don't worry." Lily looked at her mom and said, "Really, mom? Anna will be fine?" "Yes, Lily, really. Anna will be fine. She is your doll and she loves you. And I love you too," Lily's mom said. Lily smiled and said, "I love you too, mom. And I love Anna too. I'm sorry I dropped her." Lily's mom kissed Lily and Anna and said, "It's okay, Lily, it was an accident. You didn't mean to drop her. Let's go to the bench and fix Anna up, okay?" Lily nodded and said, "Okay, mom. Thank you, mom." Lily and her mom walked to the bench and fixed Anna up. They washed her dress, found her shoes and put her pants back on. They made her look pretty again. Lily hugged Anna and said, "I'm sorry, Anna. I won't drop you again. You are my best friend." Anna smiled and said, "I'm sorry, Lily. I won't fly away again. You are my best friend too." Lily and Anna were happy again. They played in the park with Lily's mom until it was time to go home. They had a fun day, even though they had a little scare. They learned to be careful and to love each other. They were a young girl and a doll, but they were also friends.

He was very curious about what was happening. On the third day, Spot was playing in the yard when he heard the sound. The big truck was back! This time, he saw a new family moving into the house next door. Spot was excited to meet them. He ran over and made new friends. The sound of the big truck had brought something good to Spot's life.

What are you doing?" The old man smiled and said, "Hello, Jane. I am just sitting here and watching the birds. I wonder what they talk about." Jane thought that was funny. She sat with the old man and watched the birds too. Suddenly, they saw smoke coming from behind a big tree. Jane and the old man were scared. They went to see what was happening. They found a small fire. The old man used his hat to put out the fire. Jane looked around and saw a little dragon hiding behind a bush. The dragon was sad because he did not know how to control his fire. Jane and the old man helped the dragon learn how to control his fire. They all became friends and had a lot of fun together. The old man was not lonely anymore, and the little dragon was happy to have friends who cared about him.

Once upon a time there lived two best friends, Ella and Hana. Every day, Ella and Hana would take their toy boats for a row in the backyard pond. One day, when Hana was rowing her pink boat, it suddenly stopped moving. "My boat is not working", she said, sad and worried. Ella knew what to do. She fixed an oar in her yellow boat and said, "Don't worry, Hana, I can take you to get help". Ella was rowing very carefully and soon they arrived at the shore. "Look," Ella said, pointing to an old man in a hat. "That man looks like he knows how to fix things. Let's ask him." Hana was scared at first, but she followed Ella and they soon found out that the old man was an expert boat fixer. He listened to the girls and quickly fixed the boat. "Thanks," said Hana, relieved. From then on, Ella and Hana always made sure to be extra careful when rowing their boats in the pond. They knew that if their boat ever stopped working again, the kind old man would be there to help them.

"Help!" cried Tom. "I want to go home!" But they did not know how to get back to the park. They searched and searched but could not find their way. Once upon a time, there lived a mighty lion named Leo. He lived in a grassy savanna, surrounded by a large herd of friendly gazelle. One day, Leo decided to raise himself up onto the tallest tree in the savanna. He wanted to go higher than the other animals so he could see the beauty of his home from above. Leo had just raised himself onto the tallest tree when a neighbouring zebra came near. The zebra said to Leo, "Why do you think you're so mighty? You shouldn't be so proud of yourself!" Leo was angry. He roared loudly and said to the zebra, "I earned the right to be proud! I'm the king of this savanna, and I will raise myself up above the rest!" The zebra decided to challenge Leo. He said, "If you think you're so mighty, let's have a race! Let's see who can cross the grass faster! The winner will be crowned king of the savanna!" Leo agreed and the race began. The zebra ran quickly, but Leo was even faster. Before long, Leo had raised himself above the zebra and crossed the grass first. He had won the race and was now the undisputed king of the savanna. One day, a bee and a butterfly were talking in a beautiful garden. The bee said "I've been looking for a new home. I want to build a hive." The butterfly replied "I know the perfect place. Come with me". The bee and the butterfly flew together to a big, shady tree nearby. The bee was very pleased and decided to build her hive there. But then, a bear came along. He wanted to settle in the same place as the bee. He was very angry and started to roar. The butterfly was very scared and flew away. The bee thought carefully and said to the bear "If you leave me alone, I promise I'll find you a better home.” The bear agreed and left the bee in peace. The bee and the butterfly worked hard to find a new home for the bear. Eventually, they found a beautiful place in the flowers nearby. The bear was very happy and he settled in his new home. The bee and the butterfly were very proud and went back to build the bee's hive. From then on, the bee and the butterfly lived happily ever after in their beautiful garden.

Tim and Sue learned that sharing and being kind is important, even when mistakes happen.

Once there was a brave mouse who wanted to pretend to be a hurricane. He ran outside in the warm sunshine and started to spin around. He spun faster and faster and his little mouse body blew a strong breeze around the playground. His brave mouse tail was blowing so hard that it felt like a real hurricane! Everyone stopped playing and came to watch the brave mouse. He kept spinning and spinning until finally he stopped and looked around. He had made a big mess! The brave mouse smiled and was very proud. He had pretended very hard and now everyone was impressed. He enjoyed the attention and ran around in circles one more time, then he went off in search of his next big adventure.

Tom and Jen are in the car with Mom. They are going to the park. But there is a lot of traffic on the road. The cars are not moving. Tom and Jen are bored. "Mom, why are we not moving?" Tom asks. "Because there is a big truck that broke down. It is blocking the road. We have to wait until they move it," Mom says. "Can we play a game?" Jen asks. "Sure, what game do you want to play?" Mom says. "I spy with my little eye something purple," Jen says. Tom looks around. He sees a purple balloon on a stick. He sees a purple flower on a hat. He sees a purple sign on a shop. "Is it the balloon?" Tom guesses. "No," Jen says. "Is it the flower?" Tom guesses. "No," Jen says. "Is it the sign?" Tom guesses. "No," Jen says. "Then what is it?" Tom says. Jen points to the back of the truck. There is a big pile of purple fruit. It looks old and soft. "It is the fruit. It is rotting. It is yucky," Jen says. "Yuck, you are right. That is purple and rotting. You win the game," Tom says. Mom smiles. She sees the truck moving. The road is clear. "Look, the traffic is gone. We can go to the park now. Hooray!" Mom says. Tom and Jen cheer. They are happy. They forget about the rotting fruit. They think about the swings and the slides. They are going to have fun.

Spot played in the sun. The sun was warm and bright. One day, Spot met a new friend. The friend was a dog. The dog was big and brown. The dog's name was Buddy. Spot and Buddy played together. They were very happy.

Once upon a time there was a tiny flower. She was very lonely and wished she had friends to play with. Everyday she sat in her pot, dreaming of flowers she might meet. One hot day, the sun shone so brightly that something amazing happened! The little flower blossomed and bloomed right before her eyes. She opened her petals and felt so happy and free. As she looked around, the garden had suddenly come alive with more flowers blooming beside her. The little flower felt so excited! The other flowers taught her lots of things, from how to play games to how to make colorful patterns in the garden. They taught her how to be kind and showed her that friendship is fun. Every day was better than the last and it seemed like the little flower was blooming more and more, until one day the garden was filled with beautiful flowers of all shapes and sizes. It was the happiest day ever! People stopped by to admire the garden and the little flower, who was beaming with joy and pride. And they lived happily ever after.

The bird was her new friend. Amy asked, "What is your name, friend?" The bird tried to answer, but it was hard for him. He said, "My name is Blue." Amy wanted to help Blue. She got some food and water for him. Blue was very happy. Amy and Blue played together all day. They laughed and had fun. At the end of the day, Amy said, "I have to go to bed now." Blue answered, "Thank you for helping me, Amy." Then, Amy went to bed with a big smile on her face. She was happy to have a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big, round orange. He loved his orange very much. He took it everywhere he went. One day, Tim met a deaf dog named Max. Max could not hear anything, but he was very friendly. Tim said, "Hi, Max! Do you want to play with my orange?" Max wagged his tail and barked. Tim and Max played with the orange all day long. They rolled it on the ground and tossed it in the air. But, they played too rough and the orange broke open. Orange juice spilled everywhere. Tim was sad because his orange was spoiled. Max licked Tim's face to make him feel better. Tim smiled and said, "It's okay, Max. We can find a new orange to play with." And from that day on, Tim and Max became best friends. They played together every day and always took care of their toys so they wouldn't spoil.

The dragons stopped fighting and looked at the princess. One of the dragons said: "Well done! You are so brave! We will help you save your country. Let's go!" The princess smiled and nodded. She, the dragons, and her loyal friends all went off to save the country. Together, they worked hard and used their magical powers to make the country pretty again. Soon, the country was saved and was brighter than ever before! Everyone was so happy. The princess and her loyal friends were very proud of their hard work. They had saved the country, and it was a very happy ending!

Sue asked the rock, "Do you want to come home with me?" The rock did not say anything. But Sue thought the rock wanted to go with her. So, she picked up the rock and marched back home. When Sue got home, she showed the rock to her mom. Her mom said, "That's not a rock, it's a big egg!" Sue was very surprised. They put the egg in a warm place. The egg started to shake and crack. Out came a big, scary monster! The monster was not nice. It ran away and made a big mess. Sue was sad and her mom was mad. The pretty rock was not a rock at all, and it was a bad ending for Sue.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to climb. One day, he saw a big bathtub in his house. He thought it would be fun to climb it. Tim started to climb the bathtub. But when he got to the top, he slipped and fell. He was wet and ashamed. He did not want his mom to see him like this. So, he hid behind the bathtub. Just then, a small frog jumped out of the bathtub. The frog was lost and needed help. Tim forgot about being ashamed and helped the frog get back to its pond. The frog was very happy. The moral of the story is that we should not let our shame stop us from helping others. Sometimes, unexpected things can happen, like meeting a new friend.

One day, a frog named Tim hopped by a pond. He saw a big, tough rock. It seemed like a nice place to rest. So, Tim hopped on the rock and sat down. "Hi, frog!" said a voice. Tim looked around but saw no one. "Down here," said the voice. Tim looked down and saw a tiny bug. "I'm Bug," said the tiny bug. "I live under the rock." Tim and Bug talked and played by the pond. They had lots of fun. But then, the tough rock started to move! Tim and Bug were scared. The rock was not a rock at all! It was a big turtle named Tom. Tom said, "I'm sorry, I was sleeping. You can play on my back if you want." So, Tim and Bug played on Tom's back and they all became good friends.

One day, a little girl named Lily was playing with her plastic toys in her room. She had a big, deep box to keep all her toys in. Lily loved to play with her toys, but she did not like to clean up after. Lily's mom came into the room and said, "Lily, you need to clean your toys and put them in the deep box." Lily did not want to clean, so she said, "But, Mom, I am still playing." Her mom said, "Okay, you can play a little more, but then you must clean." While Lily played, she heard a small voice coming from her deep box. It said, "Lily, we can help you clean!" She looked in the box and saw her plastic toys moving! They were alive! The toys jumped out of the box and started to clean the room for Lily. She was so surprised and happy. Lily said, "Thank you, toys, for helping me clean!" The toys smiled and said, "You're welcome, Lily. We love to help you!" From that day on, Lily always cleaned her room with her special plastic toys.

One day, Bobby was playing in the garden when he noticed something buzzing around the flowers. It was a bee. Bobby was fascinated by the bee and wanted to learn more about it. He asked his mom, "Mom, what is that buzzing insect?" She replied, "That is a bee, dear. It is a very gifted creature." Bobby felt that he wanted to know more about this bee. So he asked his mom, "Mom, can I help improve the life of this bee?" His mom smiled and said, "Of course you can, Bobby! You can help by providing them fresh flowers and ensuring that their environment is protected from harm." After taking his mom's advice, Bobby set off to do his best to improve the life of the bee. He painstakingly planted fresh flowers for the forager bees to enjoy, refrained from harming any bees, and spread the word about how awesome and gifted the bees were. Bobby's efforts proved to be fruitful - not only did the bee population start to increase, but they also were more active and vibrant. Bobby's mom was so proud of her son's efforts and said, "Bobby, you are truly gifted." Bobby's mom awarded him with a big hug and a kiss on his forehead.

"What does this button do?" she asked her mom. Her mom said, "I don't know, but we must not touch it. We are worried it might be dangerous." Sue wanted to help her mom, so she decided to clean the button to make it look nice. As she cleaned, she accidentally pushed the button. Sue and her mom were scared. But instead of something bad happening, a big pile of toys appeared! They were so happy and played with the toys all day. And they were never worried about the button again.

Once upon a time, there was a beautiful fish who lived in the ocean. The fish loved to perform tricks for all her friends. She would jump high, spin around, and splash back into the water. One day, the beautiful fish saw a big boat on the water. She thought it would be fun to perform for the people on the boat. So, she jumped high, spun around, and splashed back into the water. The people on the boat clapped and cheered. But, something unexpected happened. The beautiful fish found a shiny, magic rock in the ocean. She touched the rock and turned into a big, beautiful bird. Now, she could perform her tricks in the sky for everyone to see. The fish-turned-bird was very happy and her friends cheered her on from the ocean below.

Ben and Mia are playing soccer in the park. They have a big ball and two small goals. Ben is very good at kicking the ball. Mia is very good at catching the ball. "Kick the ball to me, Ben!" Mia says. She runs to the goal and waits for the ball. Ben kicks the ball hard. It flies in the air. Mia jumps and tries to catch it. But the ball is too fast. It goes past Mia and into the goal. "Goal!" Ben shouts. He is happy. He runs to the ball and picks it up. "I have one score. You have zero score." Mia is sad. She does not like to lose. She walks to the goal and sits on the ground. She does not want to play anymore. "Come on, Mia. Don't be sad. It's just a game." Ben says. He walks to Mia and gives her the ball. "You can kick the ball now. Maybe you can score too." Mia looks at the ball. She looks at Ben. She sees that he is smiling. He is not mean. He is her friend. "Okay, Ben. I will try." Mia says. She stands up and holds the ball. She kicks the ball to Ben. He lies on the ground and tries to catch it. But the ball is too high. It goes over him and into the goal. "Goal!" Mia shouts. She is happy. She runs to the ball and picks it up. "I have one score. You have one score." Ben is surprised. He did not expect Mia to score. He stands up and claps his hands. He is proud of Mia. He is her friend. "Wow, Mia. That was a big kick. You are very good." Ben says. He runs to Mia and hugs her. "You are fun to play with. Do you want to play more?" Mia nods her head. She hugs Ben back. She is happy. She is his friend. "Yes, Ben. I want to play more. Let's play more." Mia says. They smile and laugh. They kick the ball to each other. They have fun. They are friends.

Tom and Lily were friends who liked to play pretend. They had a theater in their backyard, where they put on shows for their toys and parents. Tom liked to be the hero, and Lily liked to be the princess. One day, a new boy moved in next door. His name was Max, and he had a lot of cool toys. He also liked to play pretend, and he asked Tom and Lily if he could join their theater. Tom and Lily said yes, and they welcomed Max to their group. But soon, Tom noticed that Max always wanted to be the hero, and he always wanted to save Lily. Tom felt jealous, and he thought that Max was trying to take Lily away from him. He did not like that, and he wanted to make Max go away. One day, they were playing a show about a dragon and a castle. Max was the hero, Lily was the princess, and Tom was the dragon. Tom decided to make the show more exciting, and he breathed fire at Max. He used a spray bottle and some red paper to make the fire look real. "Watch out, Max!" Lily shouted. "The dragon is breathing fire at you!" Max was scared, and he ran away from the fire. He tripped and fell on the ground, and he hurt his knee. He started to cry, and he called for his mom. Tom felt bad, and he realized that he had done something wrong. He ran to Max, and he said he was sorry. "I'm sorry, Max. I was just playing. I didn't mean to hurt you. Please don't be mad at me." Max looked at Tom, and he saw that Tom was sorry. He stopped crying, and he said it was okay. "It's okay, Tom. I'm not mad at you. I know you were just playing. But you should warn me before you breathe fire at me. That was scary." Tom nodded, and he said he would warn him next time. He helped Max get up, and he gave him a band-aid for his knee. Lily came over, and she hugged them both. "I'm glad you're both okay. You're both my friends, and I don't want you to fight. Can we play together again?" Tom and Max smiled, and they said yes. They agreed to share the hero role, and to take turns saving Lily. They went back to their theater, and they played a new show. They had fun, and they learned to be nice to each other.

Jack felt embarrassed, so he put the penny back on the ground. But then his mum said, "You can keep it, Jack! Go ahead and keep it as your special penny." Jack felt so happy. He put the penny in his pocket and said a big "Thank you!" His mum smiled. "Let's go to the store and see what you can buy with that special penny, okay?" Jack nodded eagerly and repeated "Store! Store! Store!" They went to the store and Jack chose a toy car. He was feeling so proud that he had enough money to buy it. His mum was happy too. At the end of the day, Jack felt embarrassed when his mum paid the store with real money. But he was very proud that his special penny had helped them buy something.

Tim knew that something was not right. He looked at the waves and saw that they were very big. Tim thought that maybe it was not a good day to play by the sea. Tim went back to his house and told his mom about the big waves. His mom said that it was good that he came back. The bitter wind and big waves could be a sign of a big storm coming. They stayed inside and played games all day. Later, they heard the rain and the wind outside. Tim was happy that he listened to the waves and stayed safe inside his house.

Lily and Sam are twins. They like to play outside with their toys. One day, they find a big box in the yard. They open the box and see many shiny things inside. "Wow, look at this!" Lily says. She picks up a silver necklace with a star. "It is so pretty!" "Can I see?" Sam asks. He reaches for the necklace, but Lily pulls it away. "No, it is mine! I found it first!" They start to fight over the necklace. They pull and tug and shout. They do not hear their mom calling them for lunch. "Stop, stop!" a voice says. Lily and Sam look up and see a man standing by the fence. He has a long beard and a hat. He is holding a big bag. "That is my box! I left it here by mistake!" Lily and Sam are scared. They drop the necklace and run to their mom. They tell her about the man and the box. Mom goes outside and talks to the man. He is a magician. He was doing a show at the park, but he forgot his box of tricks. He came back to get it, but he saw the twins playing with his things. He is not angry. He is sorry for scaring them. He gives them each a candy and a balloon. He shows them some of his tricks. He makes a rabbit appear from his hat. He turns a scarf into a flower. He makes a coin disappear and reappear. Lily and Sam watch with wonder. They clap and laugh. They forget about the silver necklace. They like the orange balloon better. They say thank you and goodbye to the magician. They go inside with their mom and eat their lunch. They have a new story to tell.

She pushed the button and a loud noise came out of the box. It scared them both and they started to cry. Amy's mom heard the noise and came running to see what happened. She saw the purple box and asked, "Did you push the button, Amy?" Amy said, "Yes, Mommy. I'm sorry. I regret pushing the button." Her mom hugged her and said, "It's okay. We all make mistakes. The important thing is to learn from them and listen to our friends when they try to help us." From that day on, Amy learned to be more careful and listen to her friends.

Tim was sad, but he really wanted the toy car. So, he tried to climb the shelf. As Tim climbed, the shelf began to shake. The toys on the shelf started to fall. Tim was scared. He accidentally destroyed the shelf, and all the toys fell down. The shelf was broken, and the toys were everywhere. Amy heard the noise and came to see what happened. She saw the broken shelf and the toys on the floor. Tim said, "I'm sorry, Amy. I just wanted my toy car." Amy was not happy, but she knew Tim didn't mean to destroy the shelf. Together, they cleaned up the mess and fixed the shelf. From that day on, Tim always asked for help when he needed it.

Once upon a time, there was a little shy fish. His name was Tuna. Every day, he would wander around the pond, looking for new friends. He was a bit too scared to actually swim up and say hello, though. One morning, Tuna was swimming around the pond when he saw a friendly duck. He swam as close as he could but was still too shy to say anything.  The duck noticed Tuna and swam right up to him. The duck said, "Hi, Tuna! What are you doing here all alone?" Tuna was so surprised. He began to stammer, "Uh...I...I'm just swimming around, getting to know the pond." The duck said, "Well, why don't you come with me? We can explore the pond together." Tuna smiled and nodded. So from that day forward, Tuna and the duck were the best of friends. They would wander around together and explore all the different little corners of the pond. And they all lived happily ever after!

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a yellow vehicle. The yellow vehicle was a big bus. The bus liked to help people go from one place to another. One sunny day, the bus met a little boy named Tim. Tim was sad because he wanted to go to the park but he did not know how. The bus said, "Hi Tim, I can help you. I will take you to the park." Tim was happy to gain a new friend. He got on the yellow bus and they went to the park. At the park, Tim played on the swings and had lots of fun. The bus waited for Tim to finish playing. The water went up and down, up and down. The fox started to feel dizzy from all the jumping and splashing. The dizzy fox sat down by the puddle to rest. Soon, the fox felt better. The fox smiled and knew it was time to go home. The fox walked away from the puddle, happy from a day of splashing fun.

So Kim became angry and shook him until he cried and gave her the bracelet. Tim was sad because he trusted Kim and that's why he shared his secret with her. Tom and Mia are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big picture of a leopard on a wall. The leopard has many spots and yellow fur. "Wow, look at that!" Tom says. "What is its name?" "I don't know," Mia says. "Maybe it is Leo. Leo the leopard." "Leo is a good name," Tom says. "Do you think he is nice?" Mia nods. "Yes, I think he is nice. He has clear eyes. He looks happy." Tom and Mia go closer to the picture. They want to say hello to Leo. But then they hear a loud roar. It is not Leo. It is a real leopard. He is behind the wall. He is angry. "Run!" Tom and Mia shout. They run away from the wall. They are scared. The leopard jumps over the wall. He sees Tom and Mia. He chases them. He is fast. "Help!" Tom and Mia cry. They look for a place to hide. They see a big tree. They run to the tree. They climb up the tree. They hold on tight. The leopard reaches the tree. He cannot climb. He growls. He scratches the tree. He tries to get Tom and Mia. Tom and Mia hug each other. They pray. They hope the leopard will go away. They see a man. He is a zookeeper. He has a net and a dart. He sees the leopard. He throws the net over the leopard. He shoots the dart at the leopard. The leopard falls asleep. The zookeeper calls for help. He says the leopard escaped from the zoo. He says he is sorry. He says he will take the leopard back to the zoo. Tom and Mia are safe. They are happy. They thank the zookeeper. They go down the tree. They look at the picture of Leo. They smile. "Leo is nice," Tom says. "But the real leopard is not." Mia agrees. "Yes, the real leopard is not. He is mean. He is scary." They hold hands. They walk away from the wall. They go back to the park. They play. They have fun. They forget about the real leopard. They remember Leo. Leo the leopard.

The bird flew into the automobile through an open window. The bird wanted to whistle too, but it could not. The tall man saw the bird in the automobile and thought it was stuck. He went to help the bird. He opened the door and the bird flew out. The bird was happy and started to whistle the song. The man was surprised and happy too. Just then, the owner of the automobile came out of a store. He saw the tall man near his automobile and the bird whistling. The owner was not angry. He laughed and said, "You helped my bird, so you can ride in my automobile." The tall man and the bird rode in the automobile together, whistling the happy song. They became good friends and had fun every day.

Daisy was excited and bent down to touch it. But as soon as she did, she heard a loud rumble coming from the ground beneath the mint plant. Daisy was frightened and slowly started to cry. Suddenly, Daisy heard a small and gentle voice coming from the ground. "It's ok little one. I'm not going to hurt you" it said. Daisy wiped away her tears and walked closer to the mint plant. Peeking over the tiny leaves, she saw a small frog staring up at her, its eyes kind and gentle. Daisy and the frog talked for a while. She told it how she was sad that her mom and dad had moved away, and how she was lonely without them. The frog comforted her and said it was ok for her to feel like that. Afterwards, Daisy thanked the frog and told it goodbye. She began to water the mint plant and watched it grow. She knew the frog was watching too and smiling.

Tim and Jen are friends who like to play games. Sometimes they play with cars, sometimes they play with dolls, sometimes they play with puzzles. They have fun together, but they also want to win. They are competitive. One Tuesday, they decide to play a new game. It is a game with cards and pictures. They have to match the cards with the same pictures. The one who matches the most cards wins. They shuffle the cards and put them face down on the floor. Tim goes first. He turns over two cards. They have different pictures. He says, "No match." He puts them back on the floor. Jen goes next. She turns over two cards. They have the same pictures. She says, "Match!" She takes the cards and puts them in her pile. She smiles. They take turns to turn over the cards. Sometimes they match, sometimes they don't. Jen matches more cards than Tim. She has a bigger pile. Tim feels sad. He wants to win. He thinks the game is not fair. When there are no more cards on the floor, Jen counts her cards. She has ten cards. Tim counts his cards. He has six cards. Jen says, "I win! I have more cards than you!" She jumps up and down. She is happy. Tim feels angry. He says, "You cheated! You looked at the cards before!" He throws his cards on the floor. He does not want to play anymore. He does not want to be Jen's friend. Jen feels sorry. She says, "I did not cheat. I was lucky. It is just a game. It does not matter who wins or loses. We can play again. Please don't be mad at me. Please be my friend." Tim looks at Jen. He sees that she is sad too. He thinks about what she says. He knows that she is right. It is just a game. It does not matter who wins or loses. They can play again. He does not want to be mad at her. He wants to be her friend. He picks up his cards from the floor. He says, "I'm sorry. I was mean. You did not cheat. You were lucky. It is just a game. It does not matter who wins or loses. We can play again. Please be my friend." Jen smiles. She says, "I'm sorry too. I was boastful. You did not look at the cards. You were unlucky. It is just a game. It does not matter who wins or loses. We can play again. Please be my friend." They hug each other. They are friends again. They accept that they are competitive, but they also accept that they are friends. They decide to play another game. They have fun together.

One day, Wiggly was in the garden when he heard a voice say, "Come and play with me!" Wiggly looked up and saw a big, friendly bee named Buzz. Buzz asked, "Do you want to race with me, Wiggly?" Wiggly felt weak, but he wanted to try. So, they started the race. Wiggly moved very slow, inch by inch, while Buzz flew fast. As Wiggly inched along, he saw his friends cheering for him. They all shouted, "Go Wiggly, go!" Even though he was weak, Wiggly felt strong with his friends by his side. He kept moving, inch by inch, until he crossed the finish line. Buzz and all their friends clapped and cheered. Wiggly learned that even if you are weak, you can still do great things with the help of your friends.

Once upon a time there was a bear who was always very sleepy. He wanted to take a nap, but he couldn't find a good spot. One day, he heard a voice from the sky, "Bear, bear! I promise you rain!" The bear was so excited that he could take a nap. He found a spot under a tall, shady tree and laid down. The sky was a deep blue and then suddenly, it started pouring! The bear was so happy that he could finally sleep. He laid there for hours, taking a long and peaceful nap. When the bear woke up, he was feeling much better. He thanked the sky for keeping its promise, and slowly he walked away.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Susie. Every day Susie would get dressed in her nice clothes. Susie had lots of different clothes; she had pretty dresses, warm coats and comfy leggings. One day, Susie found a funny clothes and she thought it looked fit. It was a big, white dress and it had lots of little flowers on it. Susie put on the dress and made sure it was all neat and tidy. But when Susie put the dress on, something strange happened. Steam came out of the dress and made Susie so hot and sweaty! Susie quickly took the dress off, it was far too hot to keep it on. The dress was not fit for Susie; it was meant for much bigger people! So, Susie dressed in her regular clothes and played in the park with her friends.

Once upon a time, there was a polite cat named Sam. Sam had a dream to find a big, red ball. He looked and looked, but he could not find it yet. One day, Sam saw a little girl with a big, red ball. He asked her, "Can I play with your ball, please?" The little girl said, "Yes, you can play with it, but you must be careful." Sam was so happy to play with the big, red ball. He kicked it, and he rolled it. But then, the ball went too fast and hit a big tree. The ball popped and was gone. Sam was very sad. He had found the big, red ball, but now it was gone. The little girl was sad too. Sam said, "I am sorry," but the ball was still gone. The dream was over, and the day had a bad ending.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia was ready to go to a fun party with her friends. She put on her pretty dress and her favorite mask. Her mask was blue and had a big smile on it. Mia was very happy. At the party, Mia saw her friend Tom. Tom was wearing a funny mask with a big nose. Mia laughed and said, "I like your mask, Tom!" Tom smiled and said, "Thank you, Mia! I like your mask too!" They played games and ate yummy food together. When it was time to go home, Mia and Tom waved goodbye. Mia's mom asked her, "Did you have fun at the party?" Mia smiled and said, "Yes, Mom! I had so much fun with my friends and our masks!" Mia's mom smiled too, and they went home to rest after the fun day.

Lily liked to draw with her crayons. She had many colors, but her favorite was bright pink. She drew a big flower on the paper with bright pink petals and a yellow center. "Look, Mommy, I made a flower for you!" Lily said, holding up her paper. Mommy smiled and hugged Lily. "That's a very pretty flower, sweetie. Thank you for making it for me. Where did you get the bright pink crayon?" Lily pointed to the box of crayons on the table. "It was in there, Mommy. I found it under the blue one." Mommy nodded. "That's a nice crayon, Lily. But you know what? You have to be careful with crayons. They can make marks on things that are not paper. Like the wall, or the table, or your clothes." Lily looked confused. "Why, Mommy? Crayons are for drawing." Mommy took Lily's hand and walked to the wall. She pointed to a small bright pink mark on the white paint. "Do you see that, Lily? That's a crayon mark. You made it when you were drawing your flower. Crayons can make marks on the wall, and that's not good. The wall is not for drawing. It's for looking at." Lily felt sorry. She did not mean to make a mark on the wall. She wanted to make Mommy happy, not sad. "I'm sorry, Mommy. I didn't know. How can I fix it?" Mommy smiled again. She was not angry with Lily. She knew Lily was learning. She took an eraser from the drawer and gave it to Lily. "You can fix it with this, Lily. This is an eraser. It can wipe away crayon marks. Watch." She showed Lily how to rub the eraser on the wall gently. The bright pink mark faded away. Lily clapped her hands. "Wow, Mommy, it's gone! The eraser is magic!" Mommy laughed. "It's not magic, Lily. It's just a tool. But it's a very useful tool. You can use it to wipe away any crayon marks you make by mistake. But remember, only use crayons on paper, okay?" Lily nodded. She hugged Mommy and said, "Okay, Mommy. I will only use crayons on paper. And I will use the eraser if I need to. Thank you for teaching me, Mommy. I love you." Mommy kissed Lily and said, "I love you too, Lily. You are a very good and smart girl. And you make beautiful flowers."

Once upon a time, there was a wise old owl who lived in a big tree. One day, he saw a little seed on the soil. He had an idea. "I will help this seed grow," he thought. The wise owl told his friend, the sun, to shine bright and warm. He told his friend, the rain, to come and water the seed. Soon, a tiny green shoot came out of the soil. The wise owl was very happy. As the days went by, the shoot grew taller and stronger. One day, the wise owl saw a beautiful flower on the top of the shoot. The flower thanked the owl for helping it grow. The wise owl smiled and knew that his idea had worked.

Lily and Ben were playing on the farm. They liked to run and jump and see the animals. They saw a big pile of hay near the barn. It looked soft and fun. "Let's play in the hay!" Lily said. "OK!" Ben said. They ran to the hay and climbed on top. They made a fort and a tunnel and a nest. They laughed and threw hay at each other. They did not see the farmer coming. "Hey, you kids! Get off my hay!" the farmer shouted. He was angry. He needed the hay for his cows and horses. He did not like it when the hay was messy and dirty. Lily and Ben heard the farmer and felt guilty. They knew they did something bad. They did not want to make the farmer mad. They got off the hay and ran to the fence. "Sorry, sir!" Lily said. "Sorry, sir!" Ben said. They ran away from the farm and back to their house. They decided not to play in the hay again. They learned their lesson.

Lily and Max were twins who liked to bake cakes with their mom. One day, mom gave them a big mixer to help them make the batter. Lily and Max were very happy and wanted to use the mixer first. "Let me do it!" Lily said and grabbed the mixer. "No, me!" Max said and pulled the mixer. They both pulled so hard that the mixer split in two. The batter flew everywhere and made a big mess. Mom was very angry and sad. "Lily and Max, look what you did! You broke the mixer and wasted the batter. Now we can't bake a cake. How could you be so selfish and greedy?" mom said. Lily and Max felt very sorry and ashamed. They saw how gloomy mom was and how dirty the kitchen was. They wished they had shared the mixer and worked together. "Mom, we are very sorry. We were bad and silly. We didn't mean to break the mixer and ruin the cake. We love you and we love baking with you. Can we help you clean up and make a new cake?" Lily and Max said. Mom smiled and hugged them. She was still upset, but she forgave them. "I love you too, Lily and Max. You made a mistake, but you learned from it. Next time, you have to share and cooperate. That's how we can have fun and make delicious cakes. Now, let's clean up and see if we have enough ingredients for a new cake." mom said. Lily and Max nodded and helped mom clean up. They apologized to each other and promised to share and cooperate. They found some more flour, eggs, sugar, and chocolate. They made a new batter and baked a new cake. It was not as big as the first one, but it was still yummy and sweet. They enjoyed the cake with mom and felt happy and proud. The moral of the story is: Sharing and cooperating are better than being selfish and greedy.

But then, he saw something unknown in the tree. It was a pretty kite stuck in the branches. Tim wanted to help the kite. So, Tim found a long stick and tried to attach the kite to the stick. He pulled and pulled until the kite came out of the tree. Tim was very happy. He took the kite and ran to show his mom. They played with the kite all day long in the shade.

One day, she went to the park with her mom and her toy potato. At the park, Lily met a boy named Tom. Tom saw the toy potato and said, "What is that?" Lily replied, "It's my toy potato! I love to play with it." Tom asked, "Can I play with it too?" Lily said, "Yes, we can play together!" As they played, Lily accidentally threw the toy potato too hard and it struck Tom's head. Tom started to cry. Lily felt bad and said, "I'm sorry, Tom." Tom stopped crying and said, "It's okay, Lily. Let's be more careful next time." They continued to play with the toy potato and became good friends.

Once upon a time there was a kind old man who had a garden. Every morning he watered his garden and made sure it was full of life. The old man always provided food for the birds and animals in his garden. One chilly morning the old man noticed a little dog shivering and looking filthly. The old man wanted to help the poor dog so he took it inside and gave it some hot food. He also made a warm bed and put a blanket on it. "Thank you kind old man" said the dog. The old man was so pleased that he wanted to do more. So every morning he provided hot food for the dog and helped it stay clean. But one day the old man said to the dog, "It is time for you to find your own food now, I must look after my own garden." But the dog did not want to leave, so it started barking and growling angrily. The old man was so sad and angry, he was not sure what to do. So very sadly the old man had to throw the dog out of his garden. The moral of the story is that although it is nice to help others, it is even more important to look after yourself first.

Sue loved red balls, so she was happy. Sue played with the big, red ball in her dream. But she was a little careless. While playing, she kicked the ball too hard. The ball went up, up, up, and then... it turned into a bird! Sue was very surprised. The bird flew down and said, "Thank you, Sue, for setting me free! I was trapped as a ball, but now I can fly again." Sue was happy she could help. The bird gave Sue a big hug, and then they played together until Sue woke up. Sue smiled, knowing she made a new friend in her dream.

Once upon a time, there was a sad horse named Tom. He lived on a farm with his friends, but he felt lonely. One sunny day, a little girl named Sue came to the farm. She saw Tom and said, "Why are you sad, horse?" Tom looked at her and nodded his head. He wanted to tell her that he was sad because he had no one to play with. Sue smiled and said, "I will play with you, horse!" She climbed on Tom's back, and they went for a ride around the farm. They ran through the fields and laughed together. Tom was not sad anymore. As the sun began to set, Sue gave Tom a big hug. "Thank you for playing with me, horse," she said. Tom nodded his head and smiled. From that day on, Tom and Sue were the best of friends, and the sad horse was never sad again.

Lily was scared, but she decided to show her mom. Lily took the skull to her mom and said, "Mom, look what I found! What is it?" Her mom looked at the skull and said, "This is a skull, dear. It is a part of an animal that is no longer here. We should put it back where you found it." Lily and her mom went back to the woods and put the skull back. Her mom said, "We must always respect the things we find in nature. They are a part of the world we live in." Lily learned that day that it is important to be kind to nature and to leave things where they belong.

Amy was sad, so she asked her mom, "Can Max play inside with me?" Her mom thought for a moment and said, "Yes, but we need to check the thermometer first." Amy and her mom looked at the thermometer. It said it was very cold outside. Max was happy and wagged his tail. Amy's mom allowed Max to play inside. Amy and Max played all day long. They had so much fun together. Max was a very good and hairy friend. At the end of the day, Amy gave Max a big hug and said, "I love you, Max!" And Max barked happily, which meant he loved Amy too. They knew they would always be the best of friends, no matter how cold it was outside.

Once upon a time, there was a big boat with a big sail. Sam and his dad were on the boat, ready to rush across the lake. "Are you ready for a big adventure, Sam?" Daddy asked. "Yes!" Sam shouted with excitement. Daddy released the rope and the wind began to blow. The boat sailed faster and faster. Sam couldn't believe it! "Woah!" Sam said in surprise. But as the wind grew stronger, it became tougher to keep the boat in control. Daddy kept sailing, but the waves started to rock the boat back and forth. Sam started to feel scared. "Help!" Sam cried out. Daddy quickly turned to the wheel and steered the boat back to shore. He saved the day! Sam hugged his dad, feeling relieved and proud. "You're so tough, Daddy," Sam said. Daddy smiled and said, "We all need a little help sometimes, Sammy".

Once upon a time, there was a deaf girl. She was very sad because she could not hear the wind like the others. Her friend, a kind boy, wanted to help. He thought of an idea and told the girl, "Let's separate the wind and make it so you can touch it." The girl was very excited and nodded her head. The boy told her to close her eyes and reach out with her hands. He started to make wind by swaying his body back and forth. The wind got stronger and soon the girl could feel it in her hands. She opened her eyes and smiled. She could feel the wind now! She was so happy to be able to feel the wind like the others. The girl thanked her friend for helping her. The deaf girl and the kind boy became very good friends after this and went out together often to feel the wind.

They played on the swings, the slide, and even the see-saw. After a fun day at the park, Lucy and Compy became best friends. They learned that when you meet someone new, you can make a great friend. He knew that he should be patient and wait his turn. Tim decided to slow down and stay with the other cars. As the traffic started to move, Tim stayed slow and patient. He learned that being patient and waiting his turn was important. In the end, Tim was a better car because he learned to be patient. And that was the moral of the story: always be patient and wait your turn.

Once upon a time there lived a little boy named Peter. Peter loved to take baths but his parents thought he should only take baths on special occasions. So one day, when Peter asked to take a bath, his parents said 'no'. Peter felt sad, but he wished they would let him take a bath anyway. So, Peter snuck into the bathroom when his parents weren't looking. He filled the tub with water and he rolled his sleeves up so he could jump in. But, at that moment, his parents opened the door. They were embarrassed to see their little boy in the tub! Peter was embarrassed too. But, his parents explained that it was okay to take a bath, but only when it was a special occasion. Peter understood, and from then on he listened to his parents. He knew that it was not okay to disobey them. Moral of the story: We should always listen to our parents, and not disobey them.

They played together all day. They found bugs, flowers, and even a little pond with fish. But then, Tim fell down and felt pain in his knee. He started to cry. The bird flew down and said, "Don't worry, Tim. I will help you." The bird found a big leaf and put it on Tim's knee. The pain went away. Tim smiled and said, "Thank you, bird! You are a great friend." From that day on, Tim and the bird played together every day. They discovered many new things and had lots of fun. Tim knew that the bird was a reliable friend who would always be there to help him.

One day, Tim sees a big gray bird in the sky. He wants to whistle like the bird. He tries to copy the bird's sound, but he cannot. His mouth is too tight. He tries to open his mouth more, but he still cannot. He feels sad. He asks his mom for help. His mom smiles and hugs him. She says, "Whistling is hard. You need to practice. You need to relax your mouth and blow air gently. Like this." She shows him how to whistle. Tim watches and listens. He tries again. He relaxes his mouth and blows air gently. He hears a sound. He whistles! He feels happy. He whistles louder and louder. He whistles like the bird. The bird hears him and whistles back. Tim laughs and claps. He says, "Thank you, mom. I can whistle!" His mom says, "You're welcome, Tim. I'm proud of you." They whistle together.

She ran over and said, "Hello little tire, don't be scared! I'm here to help you." The tire was so relieved. "Thank you, little one! Can you make me strong again? One day, a busy bee named Bob was flying around. He saw a big tree with lots of pretty flowers. The flowers had sweet food inside. Bob wanted to get the food for his friends. He knew he needed to yield and wait for his turn. Bob saw a little ant named Amy. Amy was looking at a menu. The menu had pictures of all the flowers and their food. Bob asked, "Amy, can I see the menu too?" Amy said, "Yes, Bob! Let's find the best flower food together." They looked at the menu and picked a big red flower. Bob and Amy went to the flower and waited. Soon, it was their turn. They got the sweet food and shared with their friends. Everyone was happy and full. Bob and Amy knew that yielding and waiting was the right thing to do.

Anna was miserable. She wanted to join the egg hunt with the other kids, but she was too sick. She had a bad cough and a hot head. Her mom said she had to stay in bed and drink soup. Anna did not like soup. She liked chocolate eggs and jelly beans. She heard the other kids laughing and running outside. She looked out the window and saw them finding eggs in the grass and behind the trees. She saw her best friend Lily holding a big basket full of eggs. Anna wished she could be there with her. She felt a tear roll down her cheek. She hugged her teddy bear and closed her eyes. She hoped she would feel better soon. Suddenly, she heard a knock on the door. She opened her eyes and saw Lily standing there with a smile. She was holding her basket and a small egg in her hand. "Hi, Anna," Lily said. "I'm sorry you're sick. I wanted to bring you something. This is for you." She gave Anna the small egg. It was pink and had a sticker of a bunny on it. Anna smiled and took the egg. She felt a warm feeling in her chest. She thanked Lily and opened the egg. Inside, there was a chocolate bunny and a note. The note said: "You are my best friend. I hope you join me soon. Love, Lily." Anna felt happy. She hugged Lily and shared her chocolate bunny with her. She told Lily about her teddy bear and her soup. Lily told Anna about the egg hunt and the other kids. They talked and laughed for a while. Anna was not miserable anymore. She had a good friend who cared for her. She hoped she would join the egg hunt next year. But for now, she was glad to have Lily and her pink egg.

One day a small brown bear cub named Brownie watched his owner doing his chores in the house. Brownie wanted to help but his owner kept telling him to stay back. Brownie was not happy and he sat in the corner with his arms crossed. Suddenly, Brownie had an idea. He ran outside and found a stick. Then, he grabbed the stick with his two paws and started poking the owner from behind. The owner looked mad and he shouted at Brownie to go away. Brownie tried again, poking the owner with his stick a little harder this time. He was determined to make the owner turn around, but he was too stubborn. He just kept doing his chores, ignoring Brownie's efforts. Suddenly, the owner stopped what he was doing and looked down. He saw the stick that Brownie was holding and he knew why Brownie was trying so hard. He had seen the same type of sticks used to help farmers with their work and he decided to give Brownie a chance to help. Though the owner had been stubborn before, he smiled as he watched Brownie helping him with his chores. From then on, Brownie was happy to help around the house and the owner was always very proud.

Lily was surprised but happy. She talked to the tiny heart and they became good friends. From that day on, Lily knew that her perfect heart symbol was special and magical. And they lived happily ever after.

This story serves to show that even when things seem pretty and inviting, it is important to stay safe and trust your gut.

Tim saw a big cow in the yard. Tim thought the cow could help him make the milk taste good again. Tim asked the cow for help. The cow said, "I can make the milk taste good, but I need something from you." Tim gave the cow a toy he loved. The cow smiled and changed the milk. Now, the milk tasted good again. Tim was happy, and his mom was happy too. From that day on, Tim and the cow were good friends, and Tim never had to drink terrible milk again.

Lily and Tom were best friends. They liked to play outside in the park. They saw many things: birds, flowers, dogs, and people. Sometimes they heard a loud noise. It was a motorcycle. They did not like the motorcycle. It was too noisy and scary. One day, they were playing with their ball. They threw it and caught it and had fun. Then they heard the motorcycle again. It was coming closer and closer. They saw a man on the motorcycle. He had a big helmet and a black jacket. He looked angry. "Hey, kids, get out of the way!" he shouted. "This is my park. You can't play here." Lily and Tom were afraid. They ran to the side of the park. They hoped the man would go away. But he did not. He stopped his motorcycle and got off. He walked towards them. He had a big stick in his hand. "What are you doing here?" he asked. "Don't you know this is my park? I don't like kids. They are noisy and annoying. You have to leave. Now!" Lily and Tom did not know what to say. They wanted to go home. They wanted their mom and dad. They wanted to be safe and peaceful. They did not want to be with the man. "Please, mister, don't hurt us," Lily said. "We are sorry. We just wanted to play. We will go now. Please let us go." The man did not listen. He raised his stick and swung it at them. He hit Lily on the head. She fell down and did not move. Tom screamed. He tried to help Lily. He tried to run away. But the man was faster. He hit Tom on the leg. He fell down and cried. The man laughed. He was happy. He had scared the kids. He had hurt them. He had made them quiet. He got on his motorcycle and drove away. He did not care about Lily and Tom. He did not care if they were alive or dead. Lily and Tom did not play in the park anymore. They did not play with their ball anymore. They did not see the birds, the flowers, the dogs, or the people anymore. They did not hear the motorcycle anymore. They did not hear anything anymore. They were gone.

Once upon a time, there was a little bug named Tim. Tim was an attractive bug with shiny wings. He liked to crawl around the garden and play with his friends. One day, Tim saw a big, tasty leaf on the ground. He wanted to eat it all by himself. Tim started to crawl towards the leaf. On his way, he met his friend, Sam the snail. Sam asked, "Tim, where are you going?" Tim said, "I am going to eat that big leaf over there." Sam looked at the leaf and said, "That looks yummy. Can I eat it with you?" Tim thought for a moment and then said, "Okay, Sam. We can share the leaf." Tim and Sam crawled together to the big leaf. They ate the leaf and had a great time. After they finished, Tim said, "I am glad we shared the leaf, Sam. It was more fun eating it with you." Sam agreed and said, "Sharing is always better than having something all by yourself." From that day on, Tim and Sam always shared their food with each other and their other friends in the garden. They learned that sharing makes everyone happy and it is more fun to enjoy things together. The moral of the story is: Sharing is caring, and it is more fun to enjoy things with friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a heavy toy truck that he loved to play with. One day, while playing, Tim's toy truck got stuck under a big rock. "Oh no!" said Tim. "How will I get my truck out?" Tim tried to lift the heavy rock, but he couldn't. He tried again and again, but he could not succeed. Tim was sad and sat down, his lip began to tremble. Just then, his friend Sam came by. "What's wrong, Tim?" Sam asked. "My truck is stuck under this heavy rock, and I can't get it out," Tim replied. Sam thought for a moment and said, "Let's try together!" So, Tim and Sam both tried to lift the rock. With their combined strength, they succeeded in lifting the heavy rock, and Tim's truck was free. Tim was so happy and thanked Sam for his help. They played with the toy truck together, smiling and laughing. From that day on, Tim and Sam knew that they could succeed in anything if they worked together.

Once upon a time, in a land far away, there was a white bunny named Fluffy. Fluffy liked to play with his friends, but they lived in different parts of the land. Fluffy wanted to unite all his friends so they could play together. One day, Fluffy hopped to his friend, Tiny the bird, and said, "Let's unite all our friends so we can play together!" Tiny agreed, and they began their journey to find their other friends. As they traveled, they faced harsh winds that blew against them. But Fluffy and Tiny did not give up. They found their friends one by one, and together, they faced the harsh wind. In the end, Fluffy united all his friends, and they played happily together.

Sara and Ben are twins. They live on a farm with their mom and dad. They like to play with the animals and help with the chores. But sometimes they fight and make a mess. One day, Sara and Ben want to bake a cake for their mom's birthday. They go to the kitchen and find some eggs, flour, sugar, and butter. They mix everything in a big bowl and pour it into a pan. Then they put the pan in the oven and wait. But they forget to set the timer. They go outside and start to play with the dog. They run and jump and laugh. They do not hear the oven beep. The cake burns and smells terrible. The smoke fills the kitchen and sets off the alarm. Mom and dad hear the noise and run to the kitchen. They see the black cake and the dirty bowl. They are angry and sad. "Sara and Ben, what did you do?" mom asks. "You spoiled the cake and made a big mess. You could have started a fire. That was very dangerous and naughty." "We are sorry, mom," Sara and Ben say. "We wanted to make you a cake for your birthday. We love you." Mom and dad hug Sara and Ben. They are still angry and sad, but they love them too. They say, "We love you too, but you have to be careful and listen. Baking a cake is not a game. You have to follow the rules and clean up after yourself. Now you have to help us fix the kitchen and make a new cake." Sara and Ben nod and say, "OK, mom and dad. We will help you. We will be good and not fight. We will not spoil anything again."

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was an old man. He loved to write poems. He would sit under a big tree and think of words to put in his poems. One day, a little girl saw the old man writing a poem. She asked him, "What are you doing?" The old man said, "I am writing a poem." The little girl did not know what a poem was, so the old man read his poem to her. She liked the poem and wanted to hold it. The old man gave the poem to the little girl to hold. She was very happy. The little girl and the old man became friends. They would meet under the big tree and write poems together. They shared their poems with each other and had lots of fun.

One day, a nice cat and a rude dog were playing in the park. They liked to enjoy the sun on their skin. The cat and dog were very different, but they were still friends. Suddenly, a big ball came rolling towards them. The rude dog wanted to play with the ball. He thought it was a fun new toy. The nice cat was scared of the ball, but she didn't want to be a scaredy-cat. The rude dog ran to the ball and tried to play with it. But when he touched it, the ball popped! The nice cat laughed and laughed. The rude dog was surprised and felt a little silly. The cat and the dog learned that sometimes, unexpected things can happen, and it's okay to be scared or surprised. They continued to enjoy the sun on their skin and played together happily.

One day, a fast car went speed down the road. The car had a glass window. Inside the car, there was a boy and his dad. The boy was happy, but his dad had a serious face. The boy said, "Dad, why are you so serious?" Dad said, "There is a big race today, and I need to win." The boy wanted to help his dad. At the race, the dad's car and another car went speed. The other car was going too fast and hit the dad's car. The glass window broke. The boy was sad, but he knew his dad was okay. The dad said, "It's okay, we can fix the car." The boy smiled and said, "Next time, we will win the race together!" And they did.

One day, a little girl named Ann wanted to bake a cake. She asked her mom, "Can I bake a cake?" Her mom said, "Yes, you can. Let's bake a cake together." They went to the store on their street to buy things to make the cake. The street was dull and not many people were there. They bought eggs, flour, and sugar. Then, they went back home to start baking. Ann and her mom mixed all the things in a big bowl. They put the mix in the oven to bake. After a while, the cake was ready. It smelled yummy! Ann and her mom ate the cake together and it was very tasty. They had a fun day baking the cake.

Sue loved her spoiled towel very much. One day, Sue went to the park with her mom and dad. She played with her ball and had lots of fun. But when it was time to go home, Sue noticed that she had dropped her spoiled towel. Sue was very sad and cried. Her mom and dad looked all over the park for the dropped towel. Finally, they found it near a tree. Sue was so happy to have her spoiled towel back. She hugged it tight and promised to never drop it again.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a little bird named Tim. Tim loved to fly from tree to tree. He liked to see all the colors of the different seasons. In the fall, the leaves were red and yellow. In the winter, the trees were white with snow. In the spring, the flowers were pink and blue. In the summer, the grass was green. One day, Tim found it difficult to fly. His wing was hurt. He was sad because he could not fly and see the pretty colors. Tim sat on a branch and cried. He wanted to fly again and see the seasons change. A kind squirrel saw Tim and wanted to help. The squirrel found a big leaf and tied it to Tim's wing. Tim felt better and tried to fly. It was still a little difficult, but Tim could fly again! Tim was happy and thanked the squirrel. They became friends and flew together to see the colors of the seasons.

One day, a little cat named Kitty was playing near the kitchen. She saw a cup of coffee on the table. Kitty was curious about the cup, so she jumped on the table to look at it. She smelled the coffee and did not like it. Kitty wanted to have some fun, so she decided to scare her friend, Doggy. Kitty went to find Doggy, who was taking a nap in the living room. She saw Doggy sleeping and thought it was the perfect time to scare him. Kitty jumped on Doggy and yelled, "Boo!" Doggy woke up with a loud bark, and he was very scared. Doggy was so scared that he ran around the house, knocking things over. He ran back to the kitchen and bumped into the table. The cup of coffee fell from the table and broke on the floor. Both Kitty and Doggy got in trouble for making a mess. The moral of the story is not to scare your friends, as it can lead to bad things happening.

Today, the cat and the dog went to play in the park. They wanted to forget the quarrel and be friends again. They played with a big red ball. They were having fun and laughing. But then, something bad happened. A big bird came and took the red ball away. The cat and the dog were sad. They thought they could be friends again, but the bird took their fun away. Now, they were not friends anymore.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Jackie. She loved Wednesday because it was the day she got to play with her best friend Rebecca. One Wednesday, Jackie's mommy took her to the store to buy her a special surprise. When they got to the store, Jackie's mommy bought her a fake doll. It had pretty eyes and a pink dress. Jackie was so excited and couldn't wait to play with her new doll. Unfortunately, when Jackie got home, she realized her new doll was broken. Jackie was sad and started to cry. Her mommy tried to cheer her up, but it wasn't working. Then suddenly, Jackie's mommy had an idea. She took the doll to a special shop and asked the shopkeeper to restore it. When Jackie came back to the shop the next Wednesday, her doll was fixed! Jackie was so happy and thanked her mommy for restoring her new friend. The moral of the story is that sometimes, something fake can be restored to be even more special.

Once upon a time, there was a cat. The cat liked to move and play all day. One day, the cat saw a big red ball. The cat wanted to play with the ball. So, the cat moved fast and hit the ball with its paw. But, the ball did not move. The cat felt guilty. It thought it broke the ball. The cat was sad and did not know what to do. It sat down and looked at the ball. Then, something unexpected happened. The ball started to move! It was not a ball, but a big red balloon. The cat was not guilty after all. It was happy and played with the big red balloon all day long.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the store with his mom. Tim saw a big, shiny toy car on the shelf. He wanted the toy car so much. He asked his mom, "Can I have the toy car, please?" His mom looked at the label on the toy car. The label said it was very costly. She told Tim, "We can't buy this toy car today. It is too costly for us." Tim was sad, but he understood. As they were leaving the store, they saw a wealthy man. The man had a big smile and was very kind. He asked Tim, "Do you want the toy car?" Tim's eyes lit up with joy. The wealthy man bought the toy car for Tim. Tim said, "Thank you so much!" The man just smiled and said, "You're welcome. Enjoy your new toy car." From that day on, Tim always remembered the kind and wealthy man who helped him get the toy car. He learned that sharing and helping others can bring happiness to everyone.

They started to read the dictionary together. They were having fun. Suddenly, they heard a loud yell from the kitchen. It was Sue's mom. Sue and Tim ran to see what happened. They were scared. But when they got to the kitchen, they saw that Sue's mom had found a big, yummy cake. She yelled because she was so happy. Sue, Tim, and Sue's mom laughed and ate the cake together. The dictionary was not scary anymore.

He saw the bird and shouted, "I'm sorry, bird!" But the bird flew away again. Tim was very sad and went home. He did not see the bird again.

Once upon a time, there was a big red barn on a wealthy farm. The barn was home to many animals like cows, pigs, and chickens. They all lived happily together. One day, a little boy named Tim came to visit the barn. Tim saw a cow and asked, "Hello, cow! What is your name?" The cow answered, "My name is Daisy. I live in this barn with my friends." Tim was very happy to meet Daisy and wanted to meet more animals. Next, Tim met a pig named Pete. "Hi, Pete! Do you like living in this big red barn?" Tim asked. Pete answered, "Yes, I love it here! We have lots of food and space to play." Tim smiled and continued to explore the barn, making new friends with all the animals.

One day, Mia went to the park to play. She saw her friend Tom. "Look at my pretty bracelet!" Mia said to Tom. But then, something unexpected happened. The bracelet fell off her wrist and rolled away. Mia and Tom were sad. Mia and Tom looked for the bracelet. They turned around and saw a big dog. The dog had the bracelet in its mouth! The dog gave the bracelet back to Mia. Mia and Tom were so happy. They played with the big dog and had a fun day at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a furry tiger. The tiger liked to play in the big forest. One day, the tiger met a little boy. The boy was not scared of the big furry tiger. The boy said, "Hi, furry tiger! Do you want to play with me?" The furry tiger and the boy played all day. They played with a big ball and ran around. The sun began to go down, and the boy said, "I need to go home now, furry tiger. You can stay here in the forest." The furry tiger did not want to stay alone in the forest. So, the tiger said, "Can I come home with you, boy?" The boy thought about it and said, "Okay, furry tiger, you can come with me. But you have to be very good." When they got to the boy's house, the furry tiger was not a tiger anymore! The furry tiger turned into a big, soft, and cuddly teddy bear. The boy was so surprised and happy. From that day on, the boy and his furry friend lived happily ever after.

One day, a humble cat named Tom went for a walk. He saw a big house with a pretty curtain in the window. Tom was curious and wanted to see what was behind the curtain. So, he jumped up and pulled it down with his paw. Behind the curtain, Tom found a magic button. He pressed it and the room started to turn. Round and round it went, faster and faster. Tom was scared and cried, "Help! Please make it stop!" Just then, a friendly dog named Max came running in. He heard Tom's cry for help and knew just what to do. Max pressed the button again, and the room stopped turning. Tom was so happy and thanked Max for saving him. From that day on, Tom and Max became the best of friends, and they never touched the magic button again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue had a puppy named Max. Max was a good and reliable puppy. He always stayed by Sue's side and played with her. One day, Sue wanted to play with a toy that her friend had. She asked her friend, "Can I borrow your toy, please?" Her friend said yes, and they played together. Max, the puppy, watched them play and wagged his tail. After they played, Sue gave the toy back to her friend. Her friend was happy that Sue was a good friend. Sue, her friend, and Max the reliable puppy all played together and had a great day.
